+# Ghost Pepper Dogfood Project
+
+This project uses the **iterative-development** methodology.
+
+## Skill Location
+
+All skills are at `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/`:
+
+- **Orchestrator:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/iterative-development/SKILL.md`
+- **Extracting requirements:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/extracting-requirements/SKILL.md`
+- **Scoping the simplest core:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/scoping-the-simplest-core/SKILL.md`
+- **Running an iteration:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/running-an-iteration/SKILL.md`
+- **Implementing tasks:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/implementing-tasks/SKILL.md`
+- **Auditing progress:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/auditing-progress/SKILL.md`
+- **PAR methodology:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/shared/parallel-adversarial-review.md`
+- **PAR wrapper:** `~/.codex/skills/iterative-development/skills/shared/par-reviewer-wrapper.md`
+
+## Script References
+
+Each skill has its own `scripts/` directory next to its SKILL.md. When a skill references `scripts/foo.py`, run it as:
+
+```bash
+python3 <skill-directory>/scripts/foo.py <args>
+```
+
+## Spec Location
+
+Human-provided spec collateral is in `specs/` in this project directory.

+# Sanitization Report
+
+## Overview
+
+All tainted specifications have been rewritten as clean behavioral specifications. Every sentence was rewritten from understanding — no text was copied from tainted sources.
+
+## Clean Artifact Inventory
+
+### Domain Specifications (6 files)
+| Domain | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| core-pipeline | Recording session lifecycle, audio capture, format conversion, pipeline orchestration |
+| speech | Speech model catalog, transcription, speaker diarization, model management |
+| text-processing | Text cleanup pipeline, correction engine, screen context capture, post-paste learning |
+| input | Keyboard hotkey monitoring, chord engine, paste mechanics, clipboard management |
+| system | Permissions, data persistence, build configuration, auto-updates |
+| user-experience | Menu bar, settings, onboarding, overlay, debug viewer, transcription lab |
+
+### Contracts (11 files)
+- cli.md — Command-line probe tool interface
+- environment.md — System environment and permissions
+- configuration.md — All user-configurable settings and file formats
+- keyboard-interface.md — Keyboard shortcut contract
+- behavioral-integration.md — Cross-domain integration requirements
+- whisperkit-integration.md — WhisperKit external system contract
+- fluidaudio-integration.md — FluidAudio external system contract
+- llm-swift-integration.md — LLM.swift external system contract
+- sparkle-integration.md — Sparkle update framework contract
+- accessibility-api.md — macOS Accessibility API contract
+- pasteboard-protocol.md — NSPasteboard protocol contract
+
+### Journeys (10 files)
+first-run, normal-dictation, toggle-dictation, clipboard-fallback, correction-learning, settings-customization, model-management, transcription-lab, debug-investigation, app-update
+
+### Test Vectors (7 files)
+audio, cleanup, input, persistence, pipeline, transcription, UI
+
+### Acceptance Criteria (12 files)
+One per domain/module: audio-system, build-distribution, context-awareness, data-persistence, debug-observability, keyboard-input-paste, permissions-system, recording-pipeline, speech-transcription, text-cleanup, transcription-lab, ui-chrome
+
+## Room B Guidance
+
+The clean specifications describe a macOS menu bar application for local speech-to-text dictation. The application:
+
+1. Monitors keyboard input for configurable chord-based hotkeys
+2. Records audio from the selected microphone
+3. Transcribes speech using local ML models (WhisperKit or FluidAudio backends)
+4. Optionally cleans up transcribed text using a local LLM (Qwen 3.5 variants via LLM.swift)
+5. Pastes the result into the focused application via simulated keystrokes
+6. Learns from user corrections over time
+
+All processing runs locally on Apple Silicon. No data leaves the machine.
+
+### Implementation Order (suggested)
+1. Core pipeline and audio system (the recording lifecycle)
+2. Speech transcription (model management and inference)
+3. Keyboard input and paste (hotkey monitoring and output)
+4. Text cleanup (LLM pipeline and corrections)
+5. System integration (permissions, persistence, updates)
+6. User experience (UI, onboarding, debug tools)
+
+### Key External Dependencies
+- WhisperKit (speech-to-text inference)
+- FluidAudio (alternative speech backend with diarization)
+- LLM.swift (local LLM inference for text cleanup)
+- Sparkle (auto-update framework)
+- macOS frameworks: AVFoundation, CoreAudio, CoreGraphics, Vision, ScreenCaptureKit, ApplicationServices
+
+## Contamination Status
+
+All 17 contamination patterns return 0 matches. Four CC-5 hits are false positives (English word "function" in prose). References to "LLM.swift" are the external package name, preserved under the External System Exception.

+# External Protocol: macOS Accessibility API Usage Contract
+
+**Contract Type:** External system protocol (macOS Accessibility API, imposed by Apple)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- all AX element attributes, roles, notifications, and observer APIs are imposed by macOS.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Consumed API Surface Summary
+
+The application uses the macOS Accessibility API (ApplicationServices / AXUIElement) for four purposes:
+
+1. **Paste target detection** -- Determining whether the focused UI element can accept pasted text.
+2. **Continuous focus tracking** -- Real-time monitoring of which element is focused in the frontmost application.
+3. **Text extraction for post-paste learning** -- Reading text content from focused elements after paste.
+4. **Paste session capture** -- Snapshotting the frontmost app, window, and focused element state at paste time.
+
+---
+
+## 2. AX Element Attributes Consumed
+
+| Attribute | API Constant | Type | Usage |
+|-----------|-------------|------|-------|
+| Role | `kAXRoleAttribute` | String | Identify element type for paste target heuristics |
+| Enabled | `kAXEnabledAttribute` | Bool | Disqualification check (disabled elements are never paste targets) |
+| Editable | `kAXEditableAttribute` | Bool | Paste target qualification (editable = accepted) |
+| Focused | `kAXFocusedAttribute` | Bool | Focus context propagation in tree walk |
+| Selected text range | `kAXSelectedTextRangeAttribute` | AXValue | Settable text field indicator |
+| Value | `kAXValueAttribute` | String or NSAttributedString | Text content extraction |
+| Value settable | `kAXIsAttributeSettable("AXValue")` | Bool | Paste target qualification (settable value = accepted) |
+| Children | `kAXChildrenAttribute` | Array of AXUIElement | Recursive tree walk for paste target detection |
+| Focused UI element | `kAXFocusedUIElementAttribute` | AXUIElement | Query frontmost app's focused element |
+| Position | `kAXPositionAttribute` | CGPoint | Element frame for paste session capture |
+| Size | `kAXSizeAttribute` | CGSize | Element frame for paste session capture |
+| Window | `kAXWindowAttribute` | AXUIElement | Resolve window from focused element |
+
+---
+
+## 3. AX Roles Recognized
+
+Three accessibility roles are recognized as text entry roles for paste target detection:
+
+| Role | AX Constant |
+|------|------------|
+| Text field | `kAXTextFieldRole` |
+| Text area | `kAXTextAreaRole` |
+| Combo box | `kAXComboBoxRole` |
+
+---
+
+## 4. AX Notifications Observed
+
+| Notification | API Constant | Observer Context |
+|-------------|-------------|------------------|
+| Focused UI element changed | `kAXFocusedUIElementChangedNotification` | Continuous paste target monitor |
+| Focused window changed | `kAXFocusedWindowChangedNotification` | Continuous paste target monitor |
+
+---
+
+## 5. AX Observer Lifecycle
+
+### 5.1 Observer Creation and Installation
+
+For each tracked application process:
+1. Create an `AXObserver` via `AXObserverCreate` for the application's PID.
+2. Register for both `kAXFocusedUIElementChangedNotification` and `kAXFocusedWindowChangedNotification` on the application AXUIElement.
+3. Add the observer to the main run loop in `.commonModes`.
+4. Enable manual accessibility on the application element (`AXManualAccessibility`) to ensure accessibility information is available.
+
+### 5.2 Observer Reinstallation on App Switch
+
+The monitor registers for `NSWorkspace.didActivateApplicationNotification`. When a different application becomes frontmost:
+1. Uninstall the previous accessibility observer.
+2. Install a new observer for the new application's process.
+
+---
+
+## 6. Paste Target Detection Heuristics
+
+### 6.1 Disqualification
+
+Disabled elements (AXEnabled = false) are never paste targets.
+
+### 6.2 Without Focus Context (no focused ancestor)
+
+An element qualifies only if it has BOTH a selected text range attribute AND a settable value attribute.
+
+### 6.3 With Focus Context (element or ancestor is focused)
+
+An element qualifies if ANY of these conditions holds:
+- AXEditable is true
+- AXValue is settable
+- Has a text entry role (text field, text area, combo box) AND has a selected text range
+- Has a text entry role alone
+
+### 6.4 Recursive Tree Walk
+
+Detection walks the accessibility tree depth-first, up to maximum depth 12. Focused children are checked before non-focused children at each level. Focus context propagates down: once any ancestor is focused, all descendants use relaxed heuristics.
+
+### 6.5 Priority Chain
+
+1. If direct focused element query succeeds and element is a paste target: return true.
+2. If direct focused element exists but is NOT a paste target: return false.
+3. If no direct focused element: fall back to cached paste target observation (accept only if PID and window ID match frontmost app and status is "editable").
+
+---
+
+## 7. Text Extraction Protocol
+
+For post-paste learning, the system reads focused element text:
+
+1. Read `kAXValueAttribute` as String or NSAttributedString.
+2. If that fails, attempt `AXTextMarkerRange`-based extraction (for WebKit views).
+3. Walk up to 8 ancestors if the focused element itself has no text.
+
+---
+
+## 8. Permission Dependency
+
+All Accessibility API operations require the Accessibility permission. The permission check uses `AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions`:
+- With prompt option `false` for silent checks.
+- With prompt option `true` to trigger the system permission dialog.
+
+Paste target evaluation returns false immediately if accessibility permission is not granted.

@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# Behavioral Integration Requirements (BIR Table)
+
+These are the cross-domain integration contracts that a reimplementor needs to understand how domains connect. Each BIR describes a data or control flow that crosses a domain boundary. The reimplementor cannot change these independently -- both sides must agree.
+
+---
+
+## BIR Table
+
+| BIR ID | From Domain | To Domain | Integration Point | Data/Control Flow | Contract Details |
+|--------|-------------|-----------|-------------------|-------------------|------------------|
+| BIR-001 | input | core pipeline | Hotkey callbacks | Hotkey monitor fires push-to-talk start/stop, toggle-to-talk start/stop, and recording restart callbacks | Each callback is a void closure. Start callbacks trigger pipeline recording start + trace creation. Stop callbacks trigger hotkey-lifted timestamp + stop-and-transcribe flow. Restart callback conditionally clears or keeps audio buffer based on 16,000 sample threshold (~1s). |
+| BIR-002 | core pipeline | audio | Recording start/stop | Pipeline starts and stops audio recording | Start clears buffer, installs tap, starts engine. Stop waits 200ms flush delay, removes tap, stops engine, returns `[Float]` buffer. Callbacks: recording started, recording stopped, converted audio chunk. |
+| BIR-003 | core pipeline | speech | Transcription dispatch | Pipeline passes `[Float]` audio buffer to the speech transcriber | Returns `String?` (nil = no transcription). Empty buffers rejected before serialization queue. Access is serialized via semaphore + serial queue. |
+| BIR-004 | core pipeline | cleanup | Cleanup invocation | Pipeline requests cleaned transcription result with text, prompt, and model kind | Returns a result with: cleaned text, fallback flag, model call duration, post-process duration, optional diagnostic transcript. If cleanup unavailable, returns deterministic corrections only. Pipeline NEVER fails to produce output. |
+| BIR-005 | core pipeline | input | Paste delivery | Pipeline delivers text via the paste system | Paste flow: save clipboard, write text, check paste target, simulate Cmd+V (50ms pre-delay, 100ms post-delay), restore clipboard. Returns paste result (success or clipboard fallback). Lifecycle callbacks: paste start, paste end, paste session. |
+| BIR-006 | audio | speech | Audio format contract | Audio recorder produces `[Float]` at 16 kHz mono Float32 | This format is chosen for compatibility with both WhisperKit and FluidAudio. Both backends consume `[Float]` directly. |
+| BIR-007 | audio | speech | Real-time audio chunks | Converted audio chunk callback delivers incremental `[Float]` chunks | Chunks are fed to the recording session manager for both FluidAudio speech accumulation AND Sortformer diarizer processing simultaneously. |
+| BIR-008 | core pipeline | context | OCR prefetch lifecycle | Pipeline starts OCR prefetch at recording start, resolves it after recording stops, cancels it on error/termination | Start launches async background capture. Resolve awaits result and returns OCR context + elapsed time. Custom vocabulary words (from correction store) passed at start. Prefetch MUST start before recording to overlap capture with recording duration. |
+| BIR-009 | context | cleanup | OCR context to cleanup prompt | OCR window contents string fed to cleanup prompt builder | Window text truncated to max 4000 characters. Appended as `<WINDOW-OCR-CONTENT>` section with `<OCR-RULES>` instructions. Only included when frontmost window context is enabled AND context is non-nil. |
+| BIR-010 | cleanup | cleanup | Correction store to OCR custom words | Preferred transcriptions passed as custom words to OCR engine | Same terms serve dual duty: improve OCR recognition accuracy AND protect preferred terms during deterministic correction. |
+| BIR-011 | input | context | Paste target detection delegation | Paste output handler delegates to the paste target detector for AX tree inspection and continuous focus monitoring | Three-layer detection: (1) direct focused element query, (2) cached observation from paste target monitor, (3) recursive AX tree walk up to depth 12. Priority chain: direct query trusted over cache. |
+| BIR-012 | input | cleanup | Post-paste learning | Paste session data triggers the post-paste learning system | Coordinator polls focused element text for 15s (1s intervals, 2s quiescence). If narrow 1-2 word replacement detected, stores as new misheard replacement in the correction store. Requires same-app check (bundle ID match). |
+| BIR-013 | core pipeline | lab | Session archival | Pipeline archives audio buffer, OCR context, raw/corrected transcription, diarization summary to the transcription lab store | Archive includes WAV audio, all metadata fields. Capacity limited to 50 entries (oldest pruned). Archive failure logged but does NOT interrupt pipeline. |
+| BIR-014 | core pipeline | observability | Performance trace lifecycle | Pipeline creates trace on hotkey, stamps milestones at each stage, completes trace after paste end | 14 milestone timestamps/durations. Summary written to debug log as "Performance" category entry. Trace abandoned (nil without summary) on pipeline busy or recording start failure. |
+| BIR-015 | core pipeline | observability | Sensitive debug snapshot | Pipeline records raw transcription + cleanup config + window context + cleaned output as "Cleanup" category | Only captured when debug log viewer is open (sensitive data gate: viewer count > 0). Once captured, persisted to disk like all other log entries. |
+| BIR-016 | core pipeline | UI | Overlay message transitions | Pipeline sets overlay messages as it transitions through states | Sequence: Recording -> Transcribing -> Cleaning Up -> dismissed. Conditional: No Sound Detected, Clipboard Fallback, Learned Correction. Each message replaces the current one, cancelling pending auto-dismiss timers. |
+| BIR-017 | speech | core pipeline | Speaker filtering decision | Recording session manager finish returns filtered transcript + diarization summary | Pipeline checks: if coordinator exists, finalize diarization. If non-empty filtered transcript produced, use it. Otherwise fall through to full-buffer transcription. Diarization summary preserved for archival regardless. |
+| BIR-018 | core pipeline | core pipeline | Pipeline exclusivity | Pipeline owner mutex prevents concurrent live recording + lab operations | Two owners: live recording, transcription lab. Acquire returns bool. Release only if caller is current owner. Live recording silently skipped if busy. Lab throws "pipeline busy" error. Released in deferred block. |
+| BIR-019 | permissions | core pipeline | Permission gates | Microphone and Accessibility permissions gate pipeline availability | Microphone denied: status -> error, pipeline unavailable. Accessibility denied: hotkey monitor fails, status -> error. Input Monitoring denied: graceful degradation. Screen Recording denied: OCR features unavailable. |
+| BIR-020 | lab | core pipeline | Lab experiment pipeline access | Lab runner acquires/releases the pipeline mutex for re-run experiments | Same pipeline exclusivity mechanism as live recording. Lab must acquire before running transcription or cleanup. Throws "pipeline busy" if held by live recording. |
+| BIR-021 | UI | core pipeline | Settings-driven behavior changes | User toggles in Settings change pipeline behavior in real time | cleanupEnabled, frontmostWindowContextEnabled, postPasteLearningEnabled, ignoreOtherSpeakers, playSounds, speechModel, cleanupModel, chordBindings -- all write to UserDefaults and trigger live reconfiguration. |
+| BIR-022 | core pipeline | audio | Sound effect triggers | Pipeline triggers start and stop sound effects | Gated by `playSounds` setting. Start sound: "Tink". Stop sound: "Pop". Both are macOS system sounds via NSSound. |
+| BIR-023 | distribution | permissions | Sparkle updater integration | "Check for Updates" menu item triggers Sparkle update check | Sparkle lazily initialized. Auto-check every 24h. EdDSA signature verification via embedded public key. |
+
+---
+
+## Integration Invariants
+
+These are cross-cutting invariants that span multiple BIRs and MUST be maintained:
+
+### INV-001: Audio Format Consistency
+The audio format produced by the audio recorder (16 kHz mono Float32) MUST match the format consumed by the speech transcriber (WhisperKit and FluidAudio both expect 16 kHz mono Float32). Any change to either side breaks transcription.
+
+### INV-002: Pipeline Stage Ordering
+OCR prefetch MUST start before recording begins (to overlap with recording). Transcription MUST complete before cleanup begins. Cleanup MUST complete before paste. These ordering constraints are sequencing discipline, not type-system enforced.
+
+### INV-003: Pipeline Always Produces Output
+The cleanup pipeline MUST always return a result, even on LLM failure. Three fallback paths ensure this: backend unavailable, unusable output, and any other error all fall back to deterministic corrections. The paste system always has text to deliver.
+
+### INV-004: Exclusive Pipeline Access
+Only one pipeline consumer (live recording or lab experiment) may hold the pipeline at a time. Both must acquire before operating and release in a deferred block. This prevents interleaved audio capture, transcription, and cleanup operations.
+
+### INV-005: Clipboard Transparency
+The paste operation MUST save the complete clipboard state before writing transcribed text, and MUST restore it after the post-keystroke delay. The only exception is the fallback path, where the transcribed text is intentionally left on the clipboard for manual paste.
+
+### INV-006: Correction Store Dual Use
+Preferred transcriptions from the correction store are passed to BOTH the OCR engine (as custom vocabulary words to improve recognition) AND the deterministic correction engine (to protect terms from modification). These MUST be the same list.

@@ -0,0 +1,115 @@
+# External Contract: Cleanup Model Probe CLI Interface
+
+**Contract Type:** External system contract (command-line interface)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- the CLI interface is consumed by shell scripts and human operators; changing flags or interactive mode semantics breaks existing usage.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Invocation
+
+The cleanup model probe is a standalone command-line executable, separate from the main application target. It is invoked via a wrapper shell script that builds the tool if necessary and forwards all arguments.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Command-Line Flags
+
+| Flag | Required | Accepts | Default | Description |
+|------|----------|---------|---------|-------------|
+| `--model` | Yes | `fast`, `full`, or accepted identifier (e.g., `qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m`) | (none) | Selects the cleanup model to load for inference |
+| `--input` | No | Text string | (none) | One-shot input text to clean up; omit for interactive mode |
+| `--prompt` | No | Text string | Built-in default cleanup prompt | Custom system prompt for the LLM |
+| `--window-context` | No | Text string | (none) | Inline OCR window context text |
+| `--window-context-file` | No | File path | (none) | Load OCR context from a file instead of inline |
+| `--thinking` | No | `none`, `suppressed`, `enabled` | `none` | Controls LLM thinking/reasoning token behavior |
+| `--interactive` | No | Flag (no value) | (none) | Enter REPL mode for repeated inputs |
+
+---
+
+## 3. Mutual Exclusivity Constraints
+
+- `--input` and `--interactive` are mutually exclusive. Providing `--input` runs one-shot mode; providing `--interactive` enters REPL mode.
+
+- `--window-context` and `--window-context-file` are mutually exclusive. Both provide OCR context, but from different sources.
+
+---
+
+## 4. Interactive REPL Mode
+
+When `--interactive` is specified (or `--input` is omitted), the tool enters a read-eval-print loop:
+
+- Reads lines from standard input.
+- Processes each line through the cleanup pipeline.
+- Exits on nil input (EOF / Ctrl+D) or the command `:quit`.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Output Format
+
+The tool prints diagnostic information to standard output:
+
+- Model information (which model was loaded).
+- Thinking mode selection.
+- For each input: the prompt, all input/output pipeline stages, and elapsed wall-clock time.
+
+---
+
+## 6. Build and Execution
+
+The probe is built as an Xcode tool target with code signing disabled (`CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO`). It depends only on the LLM product from LLM.swift -- not WhisperKit, FluidAudio, Sparkle, or the main application target.
+
+The build script supports an environment variable `CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA` to override the default derived data location (`build/cleanup-probe-cli`).
+
+---
+
+## 7. Shared Source Arrangement
+
+The probe compiles sources from both its own directory and a shared support directory. This dual-source arrangement allows the probe to share cleanup logic (prompt building, output sanitization, deterministic corrections) with the main application.
+
+---
+
+## 8. Cross-Process Preference Reading
+
+The probe reads user-configured settings from the main application's preferences at runtime. It does this by opening the main application's UserDefaults domain using the hardcoded suite name `"com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper"` (the main application's bundle identifier). If the suite cannot be opened, the probe falls back to the standard UserDefaults domain.
+
+The probe reads the following preference keys from the main application's domain:
+
+| Key | Fallback when absent | Effect on probe behavior |
+|-----|---------------------|--------------------------|
+| `"cleanupPrompt"` | Uses the built-in default prompt; overridden entirely if `--prompt` is passed on the command line | Determines the system prompt sent to the LLM, so the probe reproduces the same cleanup behavior the user sees in the main app |
+| `"preferredTranscriptionsDraft"` | Empty string (no preferred transcriptions applied) | Populates the preferred transcription list used for deterministic pre- and post-cleanup corrections |
+| `"commonlyMisheardDraft"` | Empty string (no misheard replacements applied) | Populates the commonly-misheard replacement list used for deterministic pre- and post-cleanup corrections |
+
+**Precedence rule:** When the `--prompt` CLI flag is provided, it takes unconditional precedence over the `"cleanupPrompt"` preference value. The correction store keys (`"preferredTranscriptionsDraft"`, `"commonlyMisheardDraft"`) have no CLI override and are always read from the main application's preferences.
+
+**Access mode:** The probe performs read-only access to the main application's preferences. It never writes to the main application's UserDefaults domain.
+
+**Coupling note:** The suite name `"com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper"` is a hardcoded string literal in the probe, not derived from a shared constant or build configuration. If the main application's bundle identifier changes, the probe will silently lose access to user preferences and fall back to defaults.
+
+---
+
+## 9. Error Types
+
+The CLI defines two categories of errors that govern failure reporting.
+
+### 9.1 Argument Parsing Errors
+
+Thrown during command-line argument parsing.
+
+| Condition | Thrown When |
+|-----------|-------------|
+| Missing value | A flag that requires a value (e.g., `--model`, `--input`, `--prompt`, `--window-context`, `--window-context-file`, `--thinking`) is provided without a subsequent argument, or when `--model` is omitted entirely |
+| Unknown flag | An argument does not match any recognized flag |
+| Invalid model | The value following `--model` is not `"fast"`, `"full"`, or a recognized model identifier |
+| Invalid thinking mode | The value following `--thinking` is not one of `"none"`, `"suppressed"`, or `"enabled"` |
+| Duplicate window context source | Both `--window-context` and `--window-context-file` are supplied in the same invocation |
+| Empty input | The value following `--input` is empty or contains only whitespace |
+| Invalid input combination | Both `--input` and `--interactive` are provided in the same invocation (in either order) |
+| Failed to read file | The file specified by `--window-context-file` cannot be read (e.g., missing or unreadable) |
+
+### 9.2 Execution Errors
+
+Thrown during probe execution (after argument parsing succeeds).
+
+| Condition | Thrown When |
+|-----------|-------------|
+| Model unavailable | The requested cleanup model is not loaded and cannot be used for inference |

@@ -0,0 +1,199 @@
+# External Contract: Configuration and Persistence
+
+**Contract Type:** External system contract (UserDefaults keys, Application Support file formats)
+**Redesignable:** NO for UserDefaults key names (existing user data depends on them); NO for file format schemas (existing persisted data depends on them); NO for Application Support directory layout (model caches and archives depend on paths).
+
+---
+
+## 1. UserDefaults Keys
+
+All user preferences use the macOS standard UserDefaults system. Key names are part of the external contract because changing them would lose existing user settings on upgrade.
+
+### 1.1 Declarative Storage Keys
+
+| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
+|-----|------|---------|---------|
+| `"onboardingCompleted"` | Bool | `false` | First-run wizard completion gate |
+| `"cleanupEnabled"` | Bool | `true` | Whether LLM-based text cleanup is active |
+| `"cleanupPrompt"` | String | Default cleanup prompt (built-in) | User-customizable cleanup system prompt |
+| `"speechModel"` | String | `"openai_whisper-small.en"` | Selected speech-to-text model identifier |
+
+### 1.2 Manual Keys (Nil-Check Semantics)
+
+These keys use explicit nil-check on first read to distinguish "never set" from "explicitly set to false."
+
+| Key | Type | First-Run Default | Purpose |
+|-----|------|-------------------|---------|
+| `"cleanupBackend"` | String | `"localModels"` | Selected cleanup backend option |
+| `"frontmostWindowContextEnabled"` | Bool | `false` | Whether OCR window context is included in cleanup prompts |
+| `"postPasteLearningEnabled"` | Bool | `true` | Whether post-paste correction learning is active |
+| `"ignoreOtherSpeakers"` | Bool | `false` | Whether speaker diarization filtering is enabled |
+| `"playSounds"` | Bool | `true` | Whether audio feedback sounds play |
+
+**Contract rule:** After reading each key during initialization, the resolved value MUST be written back to ensure round-trip consistency.
+
+### 1.3 One-Shot Key
+
+| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
+|-----|------|---------|---------|
+| `"hasSetLaunchAtLogin"` | Bool | `false` | Guards one-time launch-at-login registration on first run |
+
+### 1.4 Cleanup Model Selection Key
+
+| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
+|-----|------|---------|---------|
+| `"selectedCleanupModelKind"` | String | `"qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m"` | Currently selected cleanup model variant |
+
+When the stored value is not a recognized model identifier (or absent), the system falls back to the 0.8B model.
+
+### 1.5 Keyboard Shortcut Binding Keys
+
+| Key Pattern | Type | Default |
+|-------------|------|---------|
+| `"chordBinding.pushToTalk"` | Data (JSON-encoded chord) | Right Command (54) + Right Option (61) |
+| `"chordBinding.toggleToTalk"` | Data (JSON-encoded chord) | Right Command (54) + Right Option (61) + Space (49) |
+
+Key construction: prefix `"chordBinding."` + action identifier.
+
+**Chord JSON format:** A chord binding is serialized as a JSON object containing a set of physical key codes (UInt16). An empty key set raises a decoding error.
+
+### 1.6 Correction Store Keys
+
+| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
+|-----|------|---------|---------|
+| `"preferredTranscriptionsDraft"` | String | `""` (empty) | Newline-separated list of preferred transcription terms |
+| `"commonlyMisheardDraft"` | String | `""` (empty) | Newline-separated list of `wrong -> right` replacement pairs |
+
+**Parsing rules for preferred transcriptions:**
+1. Split on newlines.
+2. Trim whitespace from each line.
+3. Filter out empty lines.
+4. Deduplicate, preserving order of first occurrence.
+
+**Parsing rules for commonly misheard replacements:**
+1. Split on newlines.
+2. Trim whitespace from each line.
+3. Filter out empty lines.
+4. Split each line on `"->"` separator.
+5. Require exactly two parts; lines with zero or more than one separator are silently excluded.
+6. Trim whitespace from both "wrong" and "right" parts.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Application Support Directory Layout
+
+Root: `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/`
+
+```
+GhostPepper/
+  debug-log.json                              -- Debug log entries (JSON array)
+  models/                                     -- Cleanup model GGUF files
+    <model-filename>.gguf                       (up to 3 files, ~535 MB to ~2.8 GB each)
+  whisper-models/                             -- WhisperKit speech models
+    models/
+      openai/
+        whisper-tiny.en/                        (~75 MB)
+        whisper-small.en/                       (~466 MB)
+        whisper-small/                          (~466 MB)
+  transcription-lab/                          -- Transcription Lab data
+    transcription-lab-index.json                -- Entry metadata index
+    transcription-lab-timings.json              -- Per-entry timing data
+    audio/                                      -- Archived audio recordings (WAV files)
+      <audio-filename>
+```
+
+FluidAudio models are stored in the FluidAudio framework's own default cache directory (not under this tree).
+
+**Directory creation contract:** Each persistence subsystem MUST create required parent directories (including intermediate directories) before writing any file. Directory creation MUST be idempotent.
+
+---
+
+## 3. File Formats
+
+### 3.1 Debug Log File (`debug-log.json`)
+
+JSON array of entry objects. Each entry:
+
+| Field | JSON Type | Content |
+|-------|-----------|---------|
+| `id` | String (UUID) | Unique entry identifier |
+| `timestamp` | Number (Date) | Creation timestamp |
+| `category` | String | One of: "Hotkey", "OCR", "Cleanup", "Model", "Performance" |
+| `message` | String | Log message text |
+
+Maximum 250 entries (oldest evicted first). Writes are atomic.
+
+### 3.2 Transcription Lab Index (`transcription-lab-index.json`)
+
+JSON array of entry objects. Each entry:
+
+| Field | JSON Key | Type | Required |
+|-------|----------|------|----------|
+| Unique ID | `id` | String (UUID) | Yes |
+| Creation timestamp | `createdAt` | Number (Date) | Yes |
+| Audio file name | `audioFileName` | String | Yes |
+| Audio duration | `audioDuration` | Number (seconds) | Yes |
+| Window context text | `windowContents` | String or null | No |
+| Raw transcription | `rawTranscription` | String or null | No |
+| Corrected transcription | `correctedTranscription` | String or null | No |
+| Speech model ID | `speechModelID` | String | Yes |
+| Cleanup model name | `cleanupModelName` | String | Yes |
+| Cleanup used fallback | `cleanupUsedFallback` | Bool | Yes |
+| Speaker filtering enabled | `speakerFilteringEnabled` | Bool | Yes |
+| Speaker filtering ran | `speakerFilteringRan` | Bool | Yes |
+| Speaker filtering used fallback | `speakerFilteringUsedFallback` | Bool | Yes |
+| Diarization summary | `diarizationSummary` | Object or null | No |
+
+Maximum 50 entries. Writes are atomic. Sorted by creation timestamp, newest first.
+
+**Custom `windowContents` serialization:** Only the plain text string is written (not the full OCR context object). On deserialization, a present string is wrapped into an OCR context value.
+
+### 3.3 Transcription Lab Timings (`transcription-lab-timings.json`)
+
+JSON dictionary mapping UUID strings to timing objects:
+
+| Field | Type | Required |
+|-------|------|----------|
+| Transcription duration | Number (seconds) or null | No |
+| Cleanup duration | Number (seconds) or null | No |
+
+String-keyed on disk; entries with invalid UUID keys are silently discarded on load.
+
+### 3.4 Audio Archive Format (WAV)
+
+Standard RIFF/WAVE with these parameters:
+
+| Parameter | Value |
+|-----------|-------|
+| Sample rate | 16,000 Hz |
+| Channel count | 1 (mono) |
+| Bits per sample | 16 (signed integer PCM) |
+| Audio format code | 1 (uncompressed PCM) |
+
+Deserialization auto-detects format: if data starts with "RIFF", it parses as WAV; otherwise as raw Float32 data.
+
+### 3.5 Cleanup Model Files (GGUF)
+
+Three Qwen 3.5 models in GGUF Q4_K_M quantization format, stored in `models/` directory:
+
+| Model Kind | Parameters | Approx Size | Max Tokens | File Source |
+|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|-------------|
+| `qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m` | 0.8B | ~535 MB | 2048 | HuggingFace (unsloth) |
+| `qwen35_2b_q4_k_m` | 2B | ~1.3 GB | 2048 | HuggingFace (unsloth) |
+| `qwen35_4b_q4_k_m` | 4B | ~2.8 GB | 4096 | HuggingFace (unsloth) |
+
+---
+
+## 4. Error Recovery Contracts
+
+### 4.1 Debug Log Recovery
+
+Corrupted or unreadable debug log results in an empty entry list. No error surfaced.
+
+### 4.2 Transcription Lab Recovery (Destructive)
+
+If either JSON file (index or timings) fails to decode, ALL lab data is permanently deleted:
+1. Delete the index file.
+2. Delete the timings file.
+3. Delete the audio directory and all contents.
+4. Return empty result.

+# External Contract: Environment Variables and System-Level Configuration
+
+**Contract Type:** External system contract (OS-level configuration)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- these are imposed by macOS and build tooling conventions.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Environment Variables
+
+### 1.1 Build-Time Environment Variables
+
+| Variable | Context | Default | Purpose |
+|----------|---------|---------|---------|
+| `CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA` | Shell, build script | `build/cleanup-probe-cli` (relative to project root) | Overrides the Xcode derived data path for the cleanup model probe CLI build |
+
+### 1.2 Test Environment Detection
+
+The application detects a test environment by checking for the presence of the `"XCTestConfigurationFilePath"` environment variable (set automatically by Xcode when running unit tests). When this variable is present, the permission checker switches to a test client that returns safe defaults instead of calling real system APIs.
+
+---
+
+## 2. macOS System Permissions (Privacy TCC)
+
+The application requires or optionally uses four macOS privacy permissions managed through the Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) framework. These are imposed by macOS and cannot be redesigned.
+
+### 2.1 Required Permissions
+
+| Permission | System API for Check | System API for Prompt | Failure Behavior |
+|------------|---------------------|----------------------|------------------|
+| Microphone | `AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .audio)` | `AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .audio)` | Initialization halts; status transitions to error with "Microphone access required" |
+| Accessibility | `AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions` (prompt: false) | `AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions` (prompt: true) | Hotkey monitor cannot start; status transitions to error |
+
+### 2.2 Optional Permissions
+
+| Permission | System API for Check | System API for Prompt | Failure Behavior |
+|------------|---------------------|----------------------|------------------|
+| Input Monitoring | `IOHIDCheckAccess(kIOHIDRequestTypeListenEvent)` | `IOHIDRequestAccess(kIOHIDRequestTypeListenEvent)` | Degrades gracefully; operates with Accessibility alone |
+| Screen Recording | `CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess()` | `CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess()` | OCR features unavailable; inline recovery prompts shown |
+
+---
+
+## 3. System Settings URL Schemes
+
+The application opens specific System Settings panes using Apple's private URL scheme. These URLs are imposed by macOS and must match exactly.
+
+| Permission | URL Scheme |
+|------------|-----------|
+| Input Monitoring | `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ListenEvent` |
+| Screen Recording | `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ScreenCapture` |
+| Accessibility | `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility` |
+| Microphone | `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Microphone` |
+
+---
+
+## 4. Entitlements
+
+The application declares exactly one entitlement:
+
+| Entitlement Key | Value | Purpose |
+|----------------|-------|---------|
+| `com.apple.security.device.audio-input` | `true` | Microphone access for voice recording |
+
+No other entitlements are declared. The app sandbox is disabled (`ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX: NO`).
+
+---
+
+## 5. Enterprise MDM Pre-Approval
+
+For managed device deployments, administrators can pre-approve accessibility permissions using:
+
+| Attribute | Value |
+|-----------|-------|
+| Bundle ID | `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper` |
+| Team ID | `BBVMGXR9AY` |
+| Pre-approvable Permission | Accessibility |
+
+---
+
+## 6. Info.plist Keys
+
+The following Info.plist keys are consumed by macOS at runtime and are part of the external contract:
+
+| Key | Value | Consumer |
+|-----|-------|----------|
+| `LSUIElement` | `true` | macOS -- makes the app a menu-bar-only application (no Dock icon) |
+| `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription` | "Ghost Pepper needs microphone access to record your voice for transcription." | macOS -- microphone permission dialog |
+| `SUFeedURL` | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper/main/appcast.xml` | Sparkle framework -- appcast feed location |
+| `SUPublicEDKey` | `aU72TC8lOwqKtnWlwAkT8z7LhIvB+LRnUNRNNw9DmDQ=` | Sparkle framework -- EdDSA public key for update verification |
+| `CFBundleDisplayName` | "Ghost Pepper" | macOS -- displayed application name |
+| `CFBundleIdentifier` | `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper` | macOS -- unique application identifier |
+| `CFBundleShortVersionString` | "1.9" (current) | macOS / Sparkle -- marketing version |
+| `CFBundleVersion` | "10" (current) | Sparkle -- build number for update comparison |
+
+---
+
+## 7. Deployment Requirements
+
+| Requirement | Value |
+|------------|-------|
+| Minimum macOS version | 14.0 (Sonoma) |
+| Required architecture | Apple Silicon (arm64) -- enforced transitively by WhisperKit/FluidAudio ML dependencies |
+| Approximate RAM | ~2 GB for speech + cleanup models |
+| Approximate disk | ~1.5 GB for model downloads on first launch |

+# External Protocol: FluidAudio Integration
+
+**Contract Type:** External dependency protocol (third-party Swift package)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- FluidAudio API surface is imposed by the package; we consume it.
+**Package source:** `https://github.com/FluidInference/FluidAudio.git`, minimum version 0.12.4
+
+---
+
+## 1. Consumed API Surface
+
+### 1.1 Speech Recognition Models
+
+| API | Usage | Context |
+|-----|-------|---------|
+| `AsrModels.downloadAndLoad(version:)` | Downloads and loads the Parakeet speech recognition model | Progress reported during download |
+
+The model descriptor MUST carry a FluidAudio variant identifier. If absent, the system fails with a configuration error.
+
+### 1.2 Speech Recognition Manager
+
+| API | Usage | Parameters |
+|-----|-------|------------|
+| `AsrManager(config:)` | Creates a speech recognition manager | Default configuration |
+| `asrManager.init(models:)` | Initializes the manager with loaded models | AsrModels instance |
+| `asrManager.transcribe(audioBuffer:source:)` | Runs speech-to-text inference | Float audio buffer, `.microphone` source indicator |
+
+Transcription result text is trimmed of whitespace. If the trimmed result is empty, the system returns nil. Artifact removal is NOT applied to FluidAudio results (only to WhisperKit results).
+
+### 1.3 Sortformer Diarization Models
+
+| API | Usage | Context |
+|-----|-------|---------|
+| `SortformerModels.load(from: .huggingFace)` | Downloads/loads Sortformer speaker diarization models | Lazily cached; once loaded, reused for subsequent coordinators |
+
+### 1.4 Sortformer Diarizer
+
+| API | Usage | Context |
+|-----|-------|---------|
+| `SortformerDiarizer(models:)` | Creates a diarizer instance from loaded models | One instance per recording session coordinator |
+| `diarizer.processChunk(audioChunk:)` | Feeds real-time audio chunks for speaker analysis | Called on each converted audio chunk during recording |
+| `diarizer.finalize()` | Produces the speaker timeline | Called when recording stops |
+| Diarizer output segments | Array of segments grouped by speaker index, each with start/end times | Converted to flat diarization span array |
+| `diarizer` cleanup | Resources released after finalization | Called in coordinator's finish path |
+
+---
+
+## 2. Diarization Span Conversion
+
+Raw diarizer output (segments grouped by speaker index) is converted to flat spans:
+- Speaker identifiers synthesized as "Speaker 0", "Speaker 1", etc. (zero-indexed).
+- Resulting spans sorted by start time, with ties broken by end time.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Model Cache
+
+FluidAudio manages its own model cache directory. The exact filesystem path is controlled by the FluidAudio framework and is NOT under the application's `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/` tree.
+
+### 3.1 Cache Check
+
+To check whether a FluidAudio model is already cached, the system delegates to the FluidAudio framework's own cache directory mechanism.
+
+---
+
+## 4. Model Catalog (FluidAudio Variant)
+
+One FluidAudio model is consumed:
+
+| App Model ID | Variant | Size | Languages | Speaker Filtering |
+|-------------|---------|------|-----------|-------------------|
+| `fluid_parakeet-v3` | FluidAudio variant identifier (carried in model descriptor) | ~1.4 GB | 25 languages | YES |
+
+This is the only model in the catalog that supports speaker filtering.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Error Handling Contract
+
+| Condition | Behavior |
+|-----------|----------|
+| Transcription error | Caught, logged, return nil (no retry) |
+| Missing variant identifier in model descriptor | Fail with configuration error (code 500) |
+| Download timeout | Retry once after 500ms delay |
+| Non-timeout download/load failure | Transition to error state |
+| Per-chunk diarizer error | Caught, logged; processing continues with subsequent chunks |
+| Diarizer model loading failure | No coordinator produced; graceful degradation to full-buffer transcription |
+
+---
+
+## 6. Speaker Filtering Fallback Chain
+
+When speaker filtering is enabled but cannot produce a usable result, five fallback reasons cover every stage:
+
+1. **No usable speaker spans** -- no diarization spans available
+2. **No speaker reached threshold** -- no speaker's total duration met 0.5s threshold
+3. **Insufficient kept audio** -- merged kept audio below 0.75s minimum
+4. **Filtered audio extraction failed** -- sample extraction from buffer failed
+5. **Empty filtered transcription** -- transcription of extracted audio returned empty
+
+In all cases, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.

+# External Contract: Keyboard Shortcut Interface
+
+**Contract Type:** External system contract (hardware key codes imposed by macOS HID system)
+**Redesignable:** NO for key code values (imposed by macOS virtual key code table); NO for modifier flag bitmasks (imposed by IOKit HID); YES for default chord assignments and display formatting (internal design decisions).
+
+---
+
+## 1. Physical Key Codes
+
+Each physical key is represented by a macOS virtual key code (UInt16). These codes are imposed by the macOS HID subsystem and cannot be redesigned.
+
+### 1.1 Named Key Catalog
+
+| Key Code | Display Name | Category |
+|----------|-------------|----------|
+| 36 | Return | Non-modifier |
+| 48 | Tab | Non-modifier |
+| 49 | Space | Non-modifier |
+| 51 | Delete | Non-modifier |
+| 53 | Escape | Non-modifier |
+| 54 | Right Command | Modifier |
+| 55 | Left Command | Modifier |
+| 56 | Left Shift | Modifier |
+| 57 | Caps Lock | Modifier |
+| 58 | Left Option | Modifier |
+| 59 | Left Control | Modifier |
+| 60 | Right Shift | Modifier |
+| 61 | Right Option | Modifier |
+| 62 | Right Control | Modifier |
+| 63 | Fn / Globe | Modifier |
+
+Any unrecognized key code displays as "Key Code N" where N is the numeric code.
+
+### 1.2 Modifier Key Code Range
+
+Modifier keys occupy key codes 54-63. The hotkey monitor recognizes exactly these ten key codes as modifiers.
+
+### 1.3 Modifier Flag Bitmasks
+
+Each modifier key has a combined bitmask (device-specific + generic modifier flags) from `NX_DEVICE*KEYMASK` IOKit constants. A modifier is considered pressed when its combined bitmask is present in the event's flag field. This is imposed by the IOKit HID subsystem.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Chord Format
+
+A chord is a set of one or more physical keys that must be pressed simultaneously. Chords are the building blocks of keyboard shortcuts.
+
+### 2.1 Chord Invariants
+
+- A chord MUST contain at least one key. Construction with an empty key set returns nil; deserialization of an empty key set raises a decoding error.
+
+### 2.2 Display Formatting
+
+Chords are displayed as key names joined by " + " separators.
+
+**Canonical display sort order:**
+1. Command keys first
+2. Then Option
+3. Then Control
+4. Then Shift
+5. Then Fn
+6. Then all other keys last
+7. Ties within a category broken alphabetically by display name, then by key code
+
+### 2.3 Compact Symbol Format
+
+For the shortcut recorder UI, modifier keys use compact symbols with left/right suffixes (e.g., command symbol with right superscript for Right Command). Non-modifier keys fall through to their standard display name.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Default Bindings
+
+| Action | Default Chord | Key Codes |
+|--------|--------------|-----------|
+| Push-to-talk | Right Command + Right Option | {54, 61} |
+| Toggle-to-talk | Right Command + Right Option + Space | {54, 61, 49} |
+
+These defaults are used when no user preference is stored or when stored data cannot be decoded.
+
+---
+
+## 4. Chord Persistence Format
+
+Chord bindings are serialized as JSON-encoded binary data in UserDefaults. Keys follow the pattern `"chordBinding.<actionName>"`.
+
+| Key | Action |
+|-----|--------|
+| `"chordBinding.pushToTalk"` | Push-to-talk chord |
+| `"chordBinding.toggleToTalk"` | Toggle-to-talk chord |
+
+**Duplicate detection:** Before writing a new binding, the store checks all other actions for a binding with the same chord. If a conflict exists, the write is rejected with a duplicate binding error.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Recording Actions
+
+Two recording actions are defined (exhaustive):
+
+| Action | Activation | Deactivation |
+|--------|-----------|--------------|
+| Push-to-talk | All chord keys pressed simultaneously | Any chord key released |
+| Toggle-to-talk | Exact chord pressed (first activation) | Exact chord pressed again (second activation) |
+
+Push-to-talk can upgrade to toggle-to-talk mid-recording when the user adds the additional key(s) needed for the toggle chord while still holding the push chord.
+
+---
+
+## 6. CGEvent Tap Contract (macOS-Imposed)
+
+The hotkey monitor installs a global event tap with these macOS-imposed parameters:
+
+| Parameter | Value | Imposed By |
+|-----------|-------|------------|
+| Tap location | `.cgSessionEventTap` | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Tap placement | `.tailAppendEventTap` (end of chain) | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Tap mode | `.listenOnly` (passive, does not consume events) | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Event types monitored | `flagsChanged`, `keyDown`, `keyUp` | macOS CoreGraphics |
+
+The tap requires Accessibility permission. If denied, the tap creation request fails and the monitor does not start.
+
+### 6.1 Auto-Recovery
+
+If the operating system disables the event tap (due to timeout or user input overload), the tap callback detects this and immediately re-enables the tap. This compensates for macOS behavior of disabling taps that take too long to process.
+
+---
+
+## 7. Simulated Paste Keystroke Contract (macOS-Imposed)
+
+The simulated Command+V paste keystroke uses these macOS-imposed parameters:
+
+| Parameter | Value | Imposed By |
+|-----------|-------|------------|
+| Event source | `.hidSystemState` | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Virtual key code | `0x09` (V key) | macOS HID key code table |
+| Modifier flags | `.maskCommand` | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Post location | `.cghidEventTap` | macOS CoreGraphics |
+| Events created | keyDown + keyUp pair | macOS event model |
+
+If event creation fails (e.g., no Accessibility permission), the paste falls back to leaving text on the clipboard.

+# External Protocol: LLM.swift Integration (Cleanup Model Inference)
+
+**Contract Type:** External dependency protocol (forked third-party Swift package)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- LLM.swift API surface is imposed by the package; we consume it.
+**Package source:** `https://github.com/obra/LLM.swift.git`, branch `main` (fork, not upstream)
+
+---
+
+## 1. Dependency Risk Notes
+
+- The dependency uses branch tracking (`main`) rather than a version pin. Builds are not reproducible across time.
+- Points to a fork (`obra/LLM.swift`) rather than the upstream repository. Diverges from upstream release cadence.
+- The reason for forking is not documented in the project configuration.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Consumed API Surface
+
+### 2.1 Model Loading
+
+| API | Usage | Parameters |
+|-----|-------|------------|
+| `LLM(from:maxTokenCount:)` | Creates an LLM instance from a local GGUF model file | File URL, max token count (2048 or 4096 depending on model) |
+
+Returns an optional -- nil indicates initialization failure.
+
+### 2.2 Model Configuration
+
+After loading, these properties/methods are set on the LLM instance:
+
+| Property | Value | Purpose |
+|----------|-------|---------|
+| Temperature | 0.1 | Near-deterministic output |
+| System prompt | Set per-inference with the active cleanup prompt | Contextual prompt for each cleanup call |
+| Incremental update callback | No-op | Not used |
+| Postprocess callback | No-op | Not used |
+
+### 2.3 Inference
+
+| API | Usage | Parameters | Output |
+|-----|-------|------------|--------|
+| `llm.respond(to:thinking:)` | Runs text generation | Input text (XML-wrapped transcription), thinking mode | Generated text string |
+
+**Thinking mode values:**
+- `.none` -- no reasoning tokens
+- `.suppressed` -- reasoning tokens generated but not included in output (used during cleanup)
+- `.enabled` -- reasoning tokens included
+
+### 2.4 Shutdown
+
+| API | Usage |
+|-----|-------|
+| `LLM.shutdownBackend()` | Shuts down the inference backend globally |
+
+Called on application termination and when unloading models.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Inference Constraints
+
+### 3.1 Serialized Access
+
+Only one inference operation may execute at a time. Concurrent requests are serialized using a FIFO queue (implemented in the application, not by LLM.swift).
+
+### 3.2 Timeout
+
+Each LLM inference call is bounded by a 15-second timeout. The timeout is implemented by racing the inference against a timer in the application; it is not a feature of LLM.swift itself.
+
+### 3.3 Wait-for-Load
+
+If a load is requested while another load is in progress, the requester polls at 10ms intervals until the loading state resolves.
+
+---
+
+## 4. Model File Format
+
+All cleanup models use GGUF format (quantized Q4_K_M). Files are downloaded from HuggingFace (unsloth organization) via URLSession with progress reporting and stored at `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/models/<filename>.gguf`.
+
+### 4.1 Model Catalog
+
+| Kind | Parameters | Download Size | Max Tokens |
+|------|-----------|---------------|------------|
+| `qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m` | 0.8B | ~535 MB | 2048 |
+| `qwen35_2b_q4_k_m` | 2B | ~1.3 GB | 2048 |
+| `qwen35_4b_q4_k_m` | 4B | ~2.8 GB | 4096 |
+
+Default: `qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m` (0.8B Very fast).
+
+### 4.2 Legacy Name Resolution
+
+For archived recordings that stored display names, a mapping resolves legacy names back to current model kinds: "0.8B" -> 0.8B model, "2B" or "1.7B" -> 2B model, anything else -> 4B model.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Input/Output Format Contract
+
+### 5.1 Input Wrapping
+
+Text sent to the LLM is wrapped in XML tags: `<USER-INPUT>` before and `</USER-INPUT>` after the content.
+
+### 5.2 Output Sanitization
+
+LLM output is post-processed to remove reasoning artifacts:
+1. Strip complete reasoning blocks: matched `<think*>...</think*>` tag pairs (case-insensitive, dot-matches-all).
+2. Strip orphan leading reasoning tags: opening `<think*>` tag at start with no closing tag.
+3. Trim leading/trailing whitespace.
+
+### 5.3 Unusable Output Detection
+
+If sanitized output is empty or consists solely of "...", it is treated as unusable and the system falls back to deterministic corrections.
+
+---
+
+## 6. Error Handling
+
+| Condition | Behavior |
+|-----------|----------|
+| Model file not on disk | Download from HuggingFace, then load |
+| `LLM(from:)` returns nil | Transition to error state with message |
+| Inference error | Fall back to deterministic corrections; return pre-cleanup corrected text |
+| Inference timeout (15s) | Cancel inference; fall back to deterministic corrections |
+| Empty/unusable output | Fall back to deterministic corrections; diagnostic transcript preserved |
+
+In all fallback cases, the pipeline produces output -- it never fails to return cleaned text.

+# External Protocol: NSPasteboard Save/Restore Protocol
+
+**Contract Type:** External system protocol (macOS NSPasteboard API, imposed by Apple)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- the pasteboard API, type representations, and item model are imposed by macOS AppKit.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Pasteboard Instance
+
+All clipboard operations use `NSPasteboard.general` (the system general pasteboard). The pasteboard instance is injectable for testing.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Save Protocol
+
+Before pasting, the system saves the complete contents of the system clipboard.
+
+### 2.1 Save Procedure
+
+1. Iterate all clipboard items (`NSPasteboardItem` instances).
+2. For each item, read every type representation as raw `Data`.
+3. Store the complete item-type-data structure for later restoration.
+
+This preserves rich content (images, formatted text, file references) in addition to plain text.
+
+### 2.2 Empty Clipboard Handling
+
+If the clipboard is empty (no items), the save operation returns nil and no restoration will be attempted after paste.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Write Protocol
+
+The system writes transcribed text to the clipboard for pasting:
+
+1. Clear the clipboard (`clearContents()`).
+2. Write the transcribed text as a plain string type (`.string`).
+
+---
+
+## 4. Restore Protocol
+
+After the paste operation completes (post-keystroke delay), the saved clipboard state is restored:
+
+1. Clear the clipboard.
+2. Reconstruct all items with their original type representations.
+3. Write all items in a single batch operation.
+
+This means the paste operation is transparent to the user's clipboard contents. The transcribed text is only temporarily on the clipboard during the paste window.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Timing Contract
+
+The paste flow has critical timing dependencies that interact with the pasteboard protocol:
+
+| Phase | Delay | Purpose |
+|-------|-------|---------|
+| Pre-keystroke | 50 ms | After writing text to clipboard, before simulating Command+V; gives the system time to register new clipboard contents |
+| Post-keystroke | 100 ms | After simulated Command+V, before restoring clipboard; gives the target application time to read clipboard during paste handling |
+
+---
+
+## 6. Clipboard Fallback Path
+
+When paste target detection determines no suitable text input is focused:
+
+1. The transcribed text remains on the clipboard (from the write step).
+2. The original clipboard contents are NOT restored (user gets the transcribed text instead).
+3. The system reports a "copied to clipboard" result.
+4. An overlay notification informs the user: "Copied to clipboard" with "Cmd+V to paste" as secondary text.
+
+The same fallback is triggered when the simulated keystroke cannot be prepared (e.g., insufficient Accessibility permission for event creation).
+
+---
+
+## 7. Paste Lifecycle Callbacks
+
+Three lifecycle callbacks coordinate with the pasteboard protocol:
+
+| Callback | When Fired | Purpose |
+|----------|-----------|---------|
+| Paste start | Before any clipboard manipulation | Performance trace milestone |
+| Paste end | After clipboard restoration (normal) or after fallback determination | Performance trace milestone |
+| Paste session | After successful paste, during post-keystroke delay | Feeds post-paste learning with context snapshot |

+# External Protocol: Sparkle Auto-Update Integration
+
+**Contract Type:** External dependency protocol (third-party Swift package)
+**Redesignable:** NO -- Sparkle framework API, appcast format, and EdDSA signature scheme are imposed by the Sparkle project.
+**Package source:** `https://github.com/sparkle-project/Sparkle.git`, minimum version 2.6.0
+
+---
+
+## 1. Consumed API Surface
+
+| API | Usage | Context |
+|-----|-------|---------|
+| `SPUStandardUpdaterController(startingUpdater:updaterDelegate:userDriverDelegate:)` | Creates the Sparkle updater controller | Wrapped in lazy initialization; does not start until first access |
+| `SPUUpdater.automaticallyChecksForUpdates` | Property set to `true` | Enables automatic background update checks |
+| `SPUUpdater.updateCheckInterval` | Property set to `86400` (seconds) | 24-hour check interval |
+| `SPUUpdater.checkForUpdates()` | Triggers manual update check | Invoked from "Check for Updates" menu item |
+
+---
+
+## 2. Lazy Initialization
+
+Sparkle initialization MUST be deferred until first access. The updater controller is wrapped in a lazy initialization pattern so the Sparkle subsystem does not start until the application actually needs it.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Appcast Feed Contract
+
+### 3.1 Feed Location
+
+| Setting | Value |
+|---------|-------|
+| Feed URL | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper/main/appcast.xml` |
+| Configured in | `Info.plist` key `SUFeedURL` |
+
+The appcast file is committed to the `main` branch and served via GitHub raw content hosting.
+
+### 3.2 Feed Format
+
+The appcast MUST be a valid RSS 2.0 document using:
+- Sparkle XML namespace: `http://www.andymatuschak.org/xml-namespaces/sparkle`
+- Dublin Core namespace: `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/`
+
+Channel title: "Ghost Pepper". Channel link: GitHub repository URL.
+
+### 3.3 Feed Item Structure
+
+Each item MUST contain:
+
+| Element | Example Value | Purpose |
+|---------|---------------|---------|
+| `<title>` | "Version 1.9" | Human-readable version title |
+| `<sparkle:version>` | "10" | Internal build number (used by Sparkle for version comparison) |
+| `<sparkle:shortVersionString>` | "1.9" | Marketing version string |
+| `<sparkle:minimumSystemVersion>` | "14.0" | Must match project deployment target |
+| `<enclosure>` | (see below) | Download artifact |
+
+Enclosure attributes:
+- `url` -- download URL pointing to GitHub release DMG asset
+- `type` -- `application/octet-stream`
+- `sparkle:edSignature` -- EdDSA signature of the DMG
+- `length` -- file size in bytes
+
+### 3.4 Single-Item Feed
+
+The appcast currently advertises only a single item (the latest version). Historical versions are not retained in the feed.
+
+---
+
+## 4. EdDSA Signature Contract
+
+### 4.1 Signing
+
+Each DMG listed in the appcast MUST be signed with an EdDSA signature generated by the Sparkle `sign_update` tool during the build process.
+
+### 4.2 Verification
+
+The application MUST embed the public EdDSA key in its Info.plist under `SUPublicEDKey`. Sparkle uses this key to verify that downloaded DMGs have not been tampered with.
+
+| Key | Value |
+|-----|-------|
+| `SUPublicEDKey` | `aU72TC8lOwqKtnWlwAkT8z7LhIvB+LRnUNRNNw9DmDQ=` |
+
+---
+
+## 5. Update Check Behavior
+
+| Setting | Value |
+|---------|-------|
+| Automatic checks enabled | `true` |
+| Check interval | 86,400 seconds (24 hours) |
+| Manual check | Available via "Check for Updates" menu item |
+
+---
+
+## 6. DMG Distribution Contract
+
+| Attribute | Value |
+|-----------|-------|
+| DMG format | UDZO (compressed) |
+| Volume name | "GhostPepper" |
+| Contents | Application bundle + /Applications symlink |
+| Hosted at | `https://github.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper/releases/download/v{version}/GhostPepper.dmg` |
+| Current size (v1.9.0) | 20,568,770 bytes (~19.6 MB) |
+
+---
+
+## 7. Version Number Contract
+
+| Number | Info.plist Key | Current Value | Sparkle Role |
+|--------|---------------|---------------|--------------|
+| Marketing version | `CFBundleShortVersionString` | "1.9" | Displayed to users |
+| Build number | `CFBundleVersion` | "10" | Used by Sparkle for version comparison (monotonically increasing) |
+
+Both must be updated in Info.plist before creating a release build. A mismatch between Info.plist and appcast versions caused an update loop bug in v1.3.0.
