+# Test Vectors: Speech Transcription
+
+---
+
+## TV-001: Artifact Removal -- All Seven Hallucination Markers
+
+Each marker must be removed when present in Whisper backend output.
+
+| Input Text                                      | Expected Output        |
+|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------|
+| `"[BLANK_AUDIO]"`                               | `""`                   |
+| `"[NO_SPEECH]"`                                  | `""`                   |
+| `"(blank audio)"`                                | `""`                   |
+| `"(no speech)"`                                  | `""`                   |
+| `"[MUSIC]"`                                      | `""`                   |
+| `"[APPLAUSE]"`                                   | `""`                   |
+| `"[LAUGHTER]"`                                   | `""`                   |
+
+---
+
+## TV-002: Artifact Removal -- Embedded Markers
+
+| Input Text                                                 | Expected Output                      |
+|------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
+| `"Hello [BLANK_AUDIO] world"`                              | `"Hello  world"`                     |
+| `"[MUSIC] Speaking now [LAUGHTER] more text"`              | `"Speaking now  more text"`          |
+| `"  [NO_SPEECH]  "`                                        | `""`                                 |
+| `"Text before (blank audio) and after"`                    | `"Text before  and after"`           |
+| `"[BLANK_AUDIO][NO_SPEECH][MUSIC]"`                        | `""`                                 |
+| `"Clean text without artifacts"`                           | `"Clean text without artifacts"`     |
+
+The removal process replaces each artifact with empty string, then trims leading/trailing whitespace.
+
+---
+
+## TV-003: Artifact Removal Scope -- Whisper Only
+
+**Whisper backend output:** `"Hello [BLANK_AUDIO] world"`
+**After artifact removal:** `"Hello  world"` (trimmed: `"Hello  world"`)
+
+**FluidAudio backend output:** `"Hello [BLANK_AUDIO] world"`
+**No artifact removal applied:** `"Hello [BLANK_AUDIO] world"` (trimmed only)
+
+---
+
+## TV-004: Whisper Transcription -- Segment Joining
+
+**Whisper result segments:** [{text: " Hello "}, {text: " world "}, {text: " test "}]
+**Joined with spaces:** " Hello   world   test "
+**Trimmed:** "Hello   world   test"
+**After artifact removal (no artifacts):** "Hello   world   test"
+
+**Whisper result segments:** [{text: " Hello "}, {text: " [BLANK_AUDIO] "}, {text: " world "}]
+**Joined with spaces:** " Hello   [BLANK_AUDIO]   world "
+**Trimmed:** "Hello   [BLANK_AUDIO]   world"
+**After artifact removal:** "Hello      world" (trimmed: "Hello      world")
+
+---
+
+## TV-005: Empty Transcription Handling
+
+**Whisper result segments:** [] (empty array)
+**Joined:** ""
+**Trimmed:** ""
+**After artifact removal:** ""
+**Result:** nil (empty result = no text)
+
+**Whisper result segments:** [{text: " [BLANK_AUDIO] "}]
+**Joined:** " [BLANK_AUDIO] "
+**Trimmed:** "[BLANK_AUDIO]"
+**After artifact removal:** ""
+**Result:** nil (empty after cleanup = no text)
+
+**Empty audio buffer (0 samples):**
+**Result:** nil immediately (no backend invoked)
+
+---
+
+## TV-006: Speaker Selection Algorithm
+
+**Spans (sorted by start time):**
+
+| Speaker    | Start | End  | Duration |
+|-----------|-------|------|----------|
+| Speaker 0 | 0.0   | 0.3  | 0.3      |
+| Speaker 1 | 0.3   | 1.0  | 0.7      |
+| Speaker 0 | 1.0   | 1.4  | 0.4      |
+| Speaker 1 | 1.4   | 1.8  | 0.4      |
+
+**Total durations:** Speaker 0 = 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.7s, Speaker 1 = 0.7 + 0.4 = 1.1s
+**Threshold:** 0.5 seconds
+
+**Iteration order (chronological):**
+1. Span at 0.0: Speaker 0, total = 0.7s >= 0.5 -> **Selected: Speaker 0**
+
+Speaker 0 is selected because it appears first chronologically and has total duration >= 0.5s, even though Speaker 1 has more total time.
+
+---
+
+## TV-007: Speaker Selection -- No Speaker Meets Threshold
+
+**Spans:**
+
+| Speaker    | Start | End  | Duration |
+|-----------|-------|------|----------|
+| Speaker 0 | 0.0   | 0.2  | 0.2      |
+| Speaker 1 | 0.2   | 0.4  | 0.2      |
+| Speaker 0 | 0.4   | 0.5  | 0.1      |
+
+**Total durations:** Speaker 0 = 0.3s, Speaker 1 = 0.2s
+**Threshold:** 0.5 seconds
+
+Both speakers below threshold. No speaker selected.
+**Expected:** Fallback with reason "no speaker reached threshold"
+
+---
+
+## TV-008: Speaker Selection -- Chronological First Appearance Tiebreaker
+
+**Spans:**
+
+| Speaker    | Start | End  | Duration |
+|-----------|-------|------|----------|
+| Speaker 1 | 0.0   | 0.8  | 0.8      |
+| Speaker 0 | 0.8   | 2.0  | 1.2      |
+
+**Total durations:** Speaker 0 = 1.2s, Speaker 1 = 0.8s
+**Threshold:** 0.5s
+
+**Iteration:**
+1. Span at 0.0: Speaker 1, total = 0.8s >= 0.5 -> **Selected: Speaker 1**
+
+Speaker 1 is selected despite Speaker 0 having more total time, because Speaker 1 appears first.
+
+---
+
+## TV-009: Diarization Span Merging
+
+**Kept spans:**
+
+| Start | End  | Gap to Next |
+|-------|------|-------------|
+| 0.0   | 0.5  | 0.03        |
+| 0.53  | 1.0  | 0.10        |
+| 1.1   | 1.5  | --          |
+
+**Merge gap tolerance:** 0.05 seconds
+
+- Span 1 (0.0-0.5) + Span 2 (0.53-1.0): gap = 0.53 - 0.5 = 0.03 <= 0.05 -> **Merged** to (0.0, 1.0)
+- Merged (0.0-1.0) + Span 3 (1.1-1.5): gap = 1.1 - 1.0 = 0.10 > 0.05 -> **New span**
+
+**Expected merged spans:** [(0.0, 1.0), (1.1, 1.5)]
+
+---
+
+## TV-010: Diarization Span Merging -- Zero-Duration Spans Skipped
+
+**Kept spans:**
+
+| Start | End  |
+|-------|------|
+| 0.0   | 0.5  |
+| 0.5   | 0.5  |
+| 0.5   | 1.0  |
+
+Span at (0.5, 0.5) has zero duration -> skipped.
+
+- Span 1 (0.0, 0.5): first merged span = (0.0, 0.5)
+- Span 2 (0.5, 0.5): zero duration, skipped
+- Span 3 (0.5, 1.0): gap = 0.5 - 0.5 = 0.0 <= 0.05 -> merged with previous -> (0.0, 1.0)
+
+**Expected merged spans:** [(0.0, 1.0)]
+
+---
+
+## TV-011: Audio Extraction -- Sample Index Calculation
+
+**Sample rate:** 16000 Hz
+
+| Span Start | Span End | Start Index (floor) | End Index (ceil) |
+|------------|----------|---------------------|------------------|
+| 0.0        | 1.0      | 0                   | 16000            |
+| 0.5        | 1.5      | 8000                | 24000            |
+| 0.0625     | 0.125    | 1000                | 2000             |
+| 0.00003    | 0.00006  | 0                   | 1                |
+
+**Clamping example:**
+- Buffer length = 10000 samples
+- Span: start=0.5 (index 8000), end=1.0 (index 16000)
+- Clamped end: min(16000, 10000) = 10000
+- Extract indices: 8000..<10000
+
+---
+
+## TV-012: Audio Extraction -- Empty Buffer
+
+**Input:** Audio buffer = [], merged spans = [(0.0, 1.0)]
+**Expected:** nil (extraction returns nothing for empty buffer)
+
+---
+
+## TV-013: Minimum Kept Audio Check
+
+**Threshold:** 0.75 seconds
+
+| Merged kept duration | Passes? | Fallback reason                  |
+|---------------------|---------|----------------------------------|
+| 0.0 seconds         | No      | "insufficient kept audio"        |
+| 0.5 seconds         | No      | "insufficient kept audio"        |
+| 0.74 seconds        | No      | "insufficient kept audio"        |
+| 0.75 seconds        | Yes     | (no fallback)                    |
+| 1.0 seconds         | Yes     | (no fallback)                    |
+
+---
+
+## TV-014: Diarization Span Conversion from Raw Output
+
+**Raw diarizer output (segments grouped by speaker index):**
+
+Speaker index 0: [(start: 0.0, end: 0.5), (start: 1.0, end: 1.5)]
+Speaker index 1: [(start: 0.5, end: 1.0)]
+
+**Converted to flat spans:**
+
+| Speaker    | Start | End |
+|-----------|-------|-----|
+| Speaker 0 | 0.0   | 0.5 |
+| Speaker 0 | 1.0   | 1.5 |
+| Speaker 1 | 0.5   | 1.0 |
+
+**Sorted by start time (ties broken by end time):**
+
+| Speaker    | Start | End |
+|-----------|-------|-----|
+| Speaker 0 | 0.0   | 0.5 |
+| Speaker 1 | 0.5   | 1.0 |
+| Speaker 0 | 1.0   | 1.5 |
+
+Speaker identifiers: "Speaker 0", "Speaker 1" (zero-indexed).
+
+---
+
+## TV-015: Finalization Pipeline -- Empty Spans Fallback
+
+**Input spans:** []
+**Expected:** Fallback result with reason "no usable speaker spans"
+
+---
+
+## TV-016: Finalization Pipeline -- Empty Filtered Transcription Fallback
+
+**Setup:** Spans present, target speaker selected, kept audio >= 0.75s, audio extracted successfully.
+**Transcription callback returns:** "" (empty)
+**Expected:** Fallback result with reason "empty filtered transcription"
+
+---
+
+## TV-017: All Five Fallback Reasons
+
+| Fallback Reason                    | Triggered When                                              |
+|------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
+| No usable speaker spans            | No diarization spans available                              |
+| No speaker reached threshold       | No speaker's total duration >= 0.5 seconds                  |
+| Insufficient kept audio            | Merged kept audio duration < 0.75 seconds                   |
+| Filtered audio extraction failed   | Extracting samples from buffer failed or produced empty     |
+| Empty filtered transcription       | Transcription of extracted audio returned empty/whitespace  |
+
+---
+
+## TV-018: Span Duration Calculation
+
+| Start | End  | Expected Duration |
+|-------|------|-------------------|
+| 0.0   | 1.0  | 1.0               |
+| 0.5   | 0.5  | 0.0               |
+| 1.0   | 0.5  | 0.0 (max(0, -0.5) = 0.0) |
+| 0.0   | 0.75 | 0.75              |
+
+Duration = max(0, end - start)
+
+---
+
+## TV-019: Speech Model Catalog
+
+| Model ID                     | Title        | Variant     | Size     | Backend    | Speaker Filtering? |
+|-----------------------------|-------------|-------------|----------|------------|-------------------|
+| openai_whisper-tiny.en      | Speed       | tiny.en     | ~75 MB   | Whisper    | No                |
+| openai_whisper-small.en     | Accuracy    | small.en    | ~466 MB  | Whisper    | No                |
+| openai_whisper-small        | Multilingual| small       | ~466 MB  | Whisper    | No                |
+| fluid_parakeet-v3           | Parakeet v3 | 25 languages| ~1.4 GB  | FluidAudio | Yes               |
+
+**Default model:** "openai_whisper-small.en" (Accuracy)
+
+---
+
+## TV-020: Model Manager State Machine
+
+| Current State | Event                          | Next State |
+|--------------|--------------------------------|------------|
+| Idle         | Load requested                  | Loading    |
+| Error        | Load requested                  | Loading    |
+| Loading      | Load succeeds                   | Ready      |
+| Loading      | Load fails (retryable, timeout) | Loading (retry after 500ms) |
+| Loading      | Retry fails / non-retryable     | Error      |
+| Ready        | Same model load requested       | Ready (no-op) |
+| Ready        | Different model load requested  | Idle -> Loading |
+
+---
+
+## TV-021: Timeout Retry Detection
+
+| Error Description Contains    | URL Subsystem "timed out" code? | Retryable? |
+|-------------------------------|--------------------------------|------------|
+| "The request timed out."      | Yes                            | Yes        |
+| "Connection timed out"        | No, but description matches    | Yes        |
+| "Network is unreachable"      | No                             | No         |
+| Wrapped error with timeout    | Recursively found              | Yes        |
+
+Retry: exactly one retry after 500ms delay.
+
+---
+
+## TV-022: Configuration Parameters for Speaker Filtering
+
+| Parameter                    | Default Value | Purpose                              |
+|-----------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------|
+| Sample rate                  | 16000 Hz     | Converts time to sample indices      |
+| Substantial speaker threshold| 0.5 seconds  | Minimum total speaking time to qualify|
+| Minimum kept audio duration  | 0.75 seconds | Minimum merged kept audio            |
+| Merge gap tolerance          | 0.05 seconds | Maximum gap for merging spans        |

+# Acceptance Criteria: Audio Capture and Encoding
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-001: Recording captures audio in correct target format
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the audio engine is started and recording is active
+**When** audio is captured from the microphone
+**Then** the output MUST be in the target format: 16,000 Hz sample rate, 1 channel (mono), 32-bit floating point, non-interleaved. The format conversion pipeline MUST handle any input device sample rate and channel count.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-002: Recording start clears buffer and validates input format
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the recorder may contain data from a previous recording
+**When** a new recording is started
+**Then** the system MUST clear any previously accumulated audio data, query and validate the input format (sample rate > 0 and channels > 0), create a format converter, install an audio tap with ~100ms buffer size, start the engine, and fire the recording-started callback. If the input format is invalid, the system MUST throw a "no input available" error.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-003: Recording stop uses 200ms flush delay and correct sequence
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording is in progress with audio data in the buffer
+**When** recording is stopped
+**Then** the system MUST wait 200ms, then remove the tap, stop the engine, fire the recording-stopped callback, and copy and return the accumulated buffer. The buffer MUST contain all audio captured during the session.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-004: Audio buffer thread safety
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** audio is being recorded (tap callback runs on audio thread)
+**When** audio chunks arrive and the buffer is read on stop
+**Then** all reads and writes to the audio buffer MUST be protected by a mutual exclusion lock. The converted-audio-chunk callback MUST fire AFTER the lock is released (outside the critical section).
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-005: Device enumeration returns only valid input devices
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the system has multiple audio devices registered
+**When** the input device list is queried
+**Then** the result MUST include only devices that have at least one input channel AND a resolvable human-readable name. Devices failing either condition MUST be excluded.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-006: Raw float audio serialization round-trips losslessly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** an array of float audio samples
+**When** the samples are serialized to raw binary format and then deserialized
+**Then** the deserialized samples MUST be exactly equal to the original values. Deserialization of data whose length is not an exact multiple of 4 bytes MUST throw an "invalid serialized audio data" error.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-007: WAV serialization produces valid playable file
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** an array of float audio samples
+**When** the samples are serialized to WAV format
+**Then** the output MUST be a complete valid WAV file with 16,000 Hz sample rate, 1 channel (mono), 16-bit signed integer PCM, format code 1. The file structure MUST follow the RIFF/WAVE standard with correct chunk sizes.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-008: Converted audio chunk callback delivers incremental data
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the audio recorder is recording
+**When** a batch of audio samples is converted and appended to the buffer
+**Then** the converted-audio-chunk callback MUST fire with only the newly converted frames, not the entire accumulated buffer. Each chunk MUST be independently delivered via callback AND accumulated in the main buffer.
+
+---
+
+## AC-AUD-009: Sound effects play on recording lifecycle events
+**Priority:** P2
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the "play sounds" setting is enabled
+**When** a recording starts or stops
+**Then** the appropriate system sound effect MUST play for the start and stop events.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Build and Distribution
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-001: Project builds with correct targets and dependencies
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test (build verification)
+
+**Given** the project configuration is processed by the build system
+**When** the project is built
+**Then** three targets MUST be produced: the main application (type: application, bundle ID com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper), the unit test bundle (type: bundle.unit-test, hosted in the main app), and a CLI probe tool (type: tool). The main app MUST depend on all four packages (WhisperKit, FluidAudio, LLM, Sparkle). The probe MUST depend only on LLM.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-002: Deployment target is macOS 14.0 (Sonoma)
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the project configuration
+**When** the deployment target is checked
+**Then** the minimum deployment target MUST be macOS 14.0, enforced in both the project settings and the appcast minimum system version.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-003: Single entitlement: audio input device access
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the application entitlements file
+**When** entitlements are inspected
+**Then** exactly one entitlement MUST be declared: com.apple.security.device.audio-input. No app sandbox, network, or file access entitlements MUST be present.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-004: DMG build script produces distributable artifact
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the build script is executed
+**When** the build completes
+**Then** the script MUST: read version/build from Info.plist, clean prior artifacts, build release configuration, verify the app bundle exists, stage the app with /Applications symlink, create a compressed DMG (UDZO format), generate an EdDSA signature using Sparkle's signing tool (warning if not found), clean staging, and print the DMG path, size, and next steps.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-005: Appcast feed has correct format and EdDSA signature
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the appcast.xml file
+**When** its contents are validated
+**Then** it MUST be valid RSS 2.0 with Sparkle and Dublin Core namespaces. The channel title MUST be "Ghost Pepper". Each item MUST contain: title, Sparkle version (build number), short version string, minimum system version matching deployment target, and enclosure with download URL, MIME type application/octet-stream, EdDSA signature, and file size in bytes.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-006: Auto-update check interval is 24 hours with manual override
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the Sparkle updater controller is configured
+**When** the application runs
+**Then** automatic update checks MUST be enabled by default at 86,400 second (24 hour) intervals. A manual "Check for Updates" option MUST be available in the menu bar. Sparkle initialization MUST be deferred until first access via lazy wrapper.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-007: Info.plist contains correct application metadata
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the Info.plist file
+**When** its contents are inspected
+**Then** it MUST contain: bundle display name "Ghost Pepper", bundle ID com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper, LSUIElement = true (menu-bar-only, no Dock icon), SUFeedURL pointing to raw GitHub appcast, SUPublicEDKey for update signature verification, and NSMicrophoneUsageDescription explaining microphone access purpose.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-008: App sandbox disabled and hardened runtime configuration
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the project build configuration
+**When** security settings are inspected
+**Then** the app sandbox MUST be disabled because the application requires unsandboxed operation for accessibility API, screen capture, and model file writing. Hardened runtime is disabled in project configuration but is expected to be overridden at archive time for notarization.
+
+---
+
+## AC-BLD-009: Unit tests run as hosted bundle inside application process
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the test target configuration
+**When** tests are executed
+**Then** the test bundle MUST run inside the main application process (hosted test pattern) to access internal symbols. The test suite MUST use fakes, spies, and stubs (not mocks) for dependency injection.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Context Awareness
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-001: Window screenshot captures frontmost window correctly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** an application is frontmost with a visible window
+**When** the window capture service executes
+**Then** the system MUST identify the frontmost application, find the first matching window (correct process ID, layer 0, alpha > 0), capture it in single-window isolation mode at 2x frame dimensions (each dimension clamped to minimum 1 pixel), without scaling content, and with cursor hidden. If no frontmost application or matching window exists, the system MUST return nil.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-002: OCR recognition uses accurate mode with correct text ordering
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a captured screenshot image
+**When** OCR recognition is performed
+**Then** the system MUST use "accurate" recognition level with language correction enabled and Vision revision 3. Text observations MUST be sorted into natural reading order (top-to-bottom by descending vertical midpoint, left-to-right within 0.02 vertical tolerance), joined with spaces, and trimmed. If no text is recognized, the system MUST return nil. The average confidence score MUST be computed across all observations.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-003: OCR prefetch hides latency during recording
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording starts with cleanup enabled, a cleanup backend available, and frontmost window context enabled
+**When** recording begins
+**Then** the OCR prefetch MUST launch asynchronously before audio recording starts. The prefetch MUST record wall-clock start time and compute elapsed duration. On recording stop, the system MUST resolve the prefetch and use its result for the cleanup prompt. The prefetch elapsed duration MUST be recorded in the performance trace.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-004: OCR prefetch cancelled when conditions not met
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the OCR prefetch system is available
+**When** any of the three conditions fail (cleanup disabled, no cleanup backend available, or frontmost window context disabled)
+**Then** any existing prefetch task MUST be cancelled. The prefetch MUST also be cancelled on recording start failure, application state reset, and prepare-for-termination.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-005: OCR context truncated to 4000 characters in prompt
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** OCR window text has been captured
+**When** the text is included in the cleanup prompt
+**Then** the window text MUST be truncated to a maximum of 4000 characters (prefix truncation). The prompt MUST include an OCR-RULES section instructing the model to use context for disambiguation only, and a WINDOW-OCR-CONTENT section with the truncated text.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-006: Permission tolerance -- capture attempted regardless of preflight status
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the screen recording permission preflight reports unavailable
+**When** the OCR service attempts capture
+**Then** the service MUST still attempt the capture operation. The permission status MUST be used only for error diagnostic messages, not as a pre-check. On error, the message MUST distinguish between permission-related and other failures.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-007: Focused element locator evaluates paste target correctly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the accessibility API is available and the frontmost application has a focused element
+**When** paste target detection is invoked
+**Then** the system MUST walk the accessibility tree up to depth 12, prioritizing focused children, evaluating each element using the defined heuristics: disabled elements are never targets; without focus context, the element needs both selected text range and settable value; with focus context, editable attribute or settable value or text entry role qualifies the element.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-008: Custom vocabulary words used for both OCR and correction
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the user has configured preferred transcription terms in the correction store
+**When** OCR capture and cleanup processing occur
+**Then** the same preferred transcription terms MUST be passed to the OCR engine as custom vocabulary words AND used by the deterministic correction engine. They serve dual duty for both recognition accuracy and correction.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CTX-009: OCR service catches all errors and returns nil
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** either the capture or recognition step fails
+**When** an error is thrown during OCR processing
+**Then** the service MUST catch the error, log an appropriate diagnostic message, and return nil. The service MUST NOT propagate exceptions to callers.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Data Persistence
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-001: User preferences use correct default values on first run
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application is launched for the first time (no stored preferences)
+**When** manually-persisted settings are loaded
+**Then** the system MUST distinguish between "never set" (nil) and "explicitly set to false" using a key existence check. First-run defaults MUST be: cleanupBackend = "localModels", frontmostWindowContextEnabled = false, postPasteLearningEnabled = true, ignoreOtherSpeakers = false, playSounds = true. After reading, the resolved value MUST be written back immediately for round-trip consistency.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-002: Chord bindings persist as JSON with duplicate detection
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** chord bindings are stored under keys "chordBinding.pushToTalk" and "chordBinding.toggleToTalk"
+**When** a new binding is written or read
+**Then** bindings MUST be serialized as JSON-encoded data. Before writing, the system MUST validate no duplicate chord is assigned to a different action (reject with error if conflict found). If stored data cannot be decoded, the system MUST return nil and the application MUST fall back to hardcoded defaults.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-003: Application support directory layout is correct
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the application creates persistent storage
+**When** file-based storage is accessed
+**Then** all storage MUST reside under ~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/ with subdirectories: whisper-models/ for speech models, transcription-lab/ for lab entries (with audio/ subdirectory), and debug-log.json at the root. Cleanup models use the framework's own cache directory.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-004: Correction store persists two text drafts
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the user has configured correction rules in settings
+**When** the correction store reads and writes
+**Then** preferred transcriptions MUST be stored under key "preferredTranscriptionsDraft" and commonly misheard replacements under "commonlyMisheardDraft", both as newline-separated plain strings in user defaults. Empty defaults MUST be used when keys are absent.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-005: Cleanup model selection persists and falls back to default
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the user selects a cleanup model
+**When** the selection is read from preferences
+**Then** the selected model MUST be stored under key "selectedCleanupModelKind". If the stored value is not a recognized model identifier or the key is absent, the system MUST fall back to the default 0.8B model ("qwen35_0_8b_q4_k_m").
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-006: Cross-process preference access by CLI probe tool
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the CLI probe tool is running
+**When** it reads the main application's preferences
+**Then** it MUST open the main application's preference domain via the suite name "com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper". The --prompt CLI flag MUST take precedence over the stored cleanup prompt preference. The correction store MUST be constructed with the same domain defaults. All access is read-only.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-007: Declarative property wrapper keys persist immediately
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** a declarative property wrapper setting is mutated (onboardingCompleted, cleanupEnabled, cleanupPrompt, speechModel)
+**When** the property value changes
+**Then** the value MUST be written immediately to user defaults and MUST be readable on the next property access.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DAT-008: Settings written on every mutation via property observer
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** any manually-persisted setting is mutated
+**When** the property observer fires
+**Then** the value MUST be written to the preferences store immediately.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Debug and Observability
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-001: Five log categories with correct sensitivity classification
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the debug log store is initialized
+**When** log entries are recorded
+**Then** exactly five categories MUST be supported: Hotkey, OCR, Cleanup, Model, Performance. The Cleanup category MUST be classified as sensitive (recorded only through the gated method). All other categories MUST be non-sensitive (recorded unconditionally).
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-002: Sensitive data gated by live viewer presence
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the debug log store manages a live viewer count
+**When** sensitive recording is requested
+**Then** if no live viewer is open (count = 0), the sensitive entry MUST be silently discarded with no disk write. If a live viewer is open (count > 0), the entry MUST be recorded and persisted identically to a non-sensitive entry. The begin/end live viewing operations MUST increment/decrement the count (clamped to zero minimum).
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-003: Log store enforces 250-entry capacity with oldest-first eviction
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the log store has a configurable maximum capacity (default 250)
+**When** entries are recorded beyond the capacity limit
+**Then** the oldest entries MUST be removed from the beginning of the list until the count equals the maximum. Trimming MUST occur after every record operation and during initialization.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-004: Log entries persist to disk and survive restarts
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the debug log store writes entries to ~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/debug-log.json
+**When** the application restarts and a new store instance is created
+**Then** previously persisted entries MUST be loadable by the new instance. File writes MUST be atomic (write to temp file, then rename). If the storage file does not exist or contains invalid data, the store MUST start with an empty list.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-005: Performance trace summary contains all stage durations
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording pipeline session has completed with a performance trace
+**When** the trace summary is generated
+**Then** the summary MUST be a single space-separated string of key=value pairs containing: session ID, speech model ID, cleanup backend, hotkey_to_mic_live, hotkey_lift_to_mic_cold, transcription, cleanup (or "skipped"), ocr, prompt_build, model_call, post_process (all "skipped" if cleanup not attempted), paste, and total duration. Missing values MUST display as "n/a". All durations MUST be formatted in milliseconds.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-006: Log entry format uses [HH:mm:ss] [Category] message
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the log store contains entries
+**When** the formatted text output is computed
+**Then** each entry MUST be formatted as "[HH:mm:ss] [CategoryLabel] message" with entries separated by double newlines. If no entries exist, the formatted text MUST be an empty string.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-007: Clear operation removes all entries and persists empty state
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the log store contains entries
+**When** the clear operation is invoked
+**Then** all entries MUST be removed from the list and the empty state MUST be immediately persisted to disk.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-008: All file I/O errors silently suppressed
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the debug log store performs file operations
+**When** any file I/O error occurs (read, write, encode, decode, directory creation)
+**Then** the error MUST be silently suppressed. The store MUST never report persistence errors to the user or any logging system.
+
+---
+
+## AC-DBG-009: Sensitive content logged under correct four categories
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the application orchestrator produces debug entries
+**When** recording cleanup-related content
+**Then** the sensitive recording method MUST be used for exactly: raw transcription text, cleanup configuration details, window context summary, and final cleaned output -- all under the "Cleanup" category. All other categories (Hotkey, OCR, Model, Performance) MUST use the standard non-gated method.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Keyboard Input and Paste
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-001: Push-to-talk chord starts and stops recording
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the hotkey monitor is running and the chord engine is idle
+**When** the user presses and holds all keys in the push-to-talk chord simultaneously
+**Then** recording MUST start. When any single chord key is released, recording MUST stop immediately. Pressing additional non-chord keys while holding the chord MUST NOT interfere with recording.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-002: Toggle-to-talk chord starts and stops recording on separate activations
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the hotkey monitor is running and the chord engine is idle
+**When** the user presses the toggle-to-talk chord once
+**Then** recording MUST start and keys may be freely released and pressed without affecting the recording. When the user presses the exact toggle chord a second time, recording MUST stop.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-003: Push-to-toggle upgrade triggers restart
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** push-to-talk is the active recording action
+**When** the user additionally presses keys to complete the toggle-to-talk chord
+**Then** the chord engine MUST produce a "restart recording" effect and the active action MUST transition to toggle-to-talk.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-004: Clipboard save and restore preserves user clipboard
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the system clipboard contains rich content (text, images, file references)
+**When** a paste operation completes successfully
+**Then** the system MUST save all clipboard items with all type representations before pasting, write the transcribed text temporarily, simulate the paste keystroke, and then restore the original clipboard contents. The user's original clipboard MUST be preserved after the paste operation.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-005: Paste target detection prevents paste into non-editable elements
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the transcribed text is ready for paste delivery
+**When** no suitable editable text input is focused (the paste target detection returns false)
+**Then** the system MUST NOT simulate a keystroke. The transcribed text MUST remain on the clipboard (original contents NOT restored). The system MUST report "copied to clipboard" and show the clipboard fallback overlay.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-006: Simulated keystroke paste uses correct timing delays
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a paste target has been detected as editable
+**When** the paste operation executes
+**Then** the system MUST wait 50ms after writing to clipboard before simulating Command+V. After the keystroke, the system MUST wait 100ms before capturing the paste session and restoring the clipboard.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-007: Chord binding duplicate detection prevents conflicts
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a chord binding is already assigned to one action (e.g., push-to-talk)
+**When** the user attempts to assign the same chord to a different action (e.g., toggle-to-talk)
+**Then** the operation MUST be rejected with a duplicate binding error. The existing binding MUST remain unchanged.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-008: Event tap auto-recovery re-enables disabled taps
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the global event tap is installed and running
+**When** the operating system disables the tap (due to timeout or overload)
+**Then** the tap callback MUST detect the disabling and immediately re-enable the tap.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-009: Shortcut capture suspends hotkey monitor and uses release guard
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the user initiates shortcut capture in settings
+**When** the capture session begins
+**Then** the hotkey monitor MUST be suspended and the chord engine MUST be reset. When capture completes and the monitor is unsuspended, if keys are still physically pressed, chord matching MUST remain disabled until all keys are released or a fresh single key press occurs.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-010: Non-modifier event gating avoids unnecessary processing
+**Priority:** P2
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the hotkey monitor is running with configured chord bindings
+**When** the user types regular keys that do not match modifier prefixes of any configured chord
+**Then** the non-modifier key events MUST be ignored without entering the chord engine evaluation, producing zero processing overhead.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-011: Key chord requires at least one key
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a chord is being constructed or deserialized
+**When** the key set is empty
+**Then** construction with an empty key set MUST return nil (failable initializer) and deserialization of an empty key set MUST raise a decoding error.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-012: Paste session captured for post-paste learning
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a successful paste has been simulated
+**When** 100ms have elapsed after the keystroke
+**Then** the system MUST capture a paste session snapshot containing: pasted text, timestamp, frontmost app bundle identifier, window identifier and frame, and focused element frame and text content. This snapshot MUST be delivered to the post-paste learning callback.
+
+---
+
+## AC-KIP-013: Default chord bindings are correct
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** no user preference for chord bindings has been stored
+**When** the application loads chord bindings
+**Then** the default push-to-talk chord MUST be Right Command + Right Option (key codes 54, 61) and the default toggle-to-talk chord MUST be Right Command + Right Option + Space (key codes 54, 61, 49).

+# Acceptance Criteria: Permissions System
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-001: Microphone denial halts initialization with error state
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application is initializing (after onboarding)
+**When** microphone access is denied or restricted
+**Then** the system MUST set the error message to "Microphone access required", transition to the error state, and halt initialization. The pipeline MUST NOT be available for recording.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-002: Microphone permission request flow handles all three states
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the system queries microphone permission status
+**When** the status is "authorized", "not determined", or "denied"
+**Then** the combined check-and-request operation MUST: return success immediately if authorized, request access from the OS and return result if not determined, return failure immediately if denied (no prompt possible).
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-003: Accessibility failure blocks hotkey monitor with error and prompt
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application attempts to start the hotkey monitor
+**When** the hotkey monitor fails to start (accessibility permission not granted)
+**Then** the system MUST prompt for accessibility permission, set the error message to "Accessibility access required -- grant permission then click Retry", and transition to the error state.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-004: Input monitoring degrades gracefully when denied
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** input monitoring permission is not granted
+**When** the hotkey monitor is about to start
+**Then** the system MUST prompt for the permission but MUST NOT block startup. It MUST log that input monitoring was not granted and attempt to start with accessibility alone.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-005: Screen recording permission controller state machine
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the screen recording permission controller is initialized
+**When** the permission status changes
+**Then** the controller MUST initialize its grant status at construction time, provide a refresh operation that re-queries status, and provide a request-access operation that calls the request function then refreshes. The controller MUST accept injectable closures for testability.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-006: Settings show inline recovery when screen recording missing
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the "Use frontmost window OCR context" or "Learn from manual corrections after paste" feature is enabled
+**When** screen recording permission is not granted
+**Then** the settings MUST display an inline recovery prompt with the text "Ghost Pepper needs Screen Recording access. Grant it in System Settings, then return to Ghost Pepper." with an "Open Screen Recording Settings" button.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-007: Onboarding setup step permission flow
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user is on the onboarding setup step (step 2)
+**When** the four setup items are presented (microphone, accessibility, screen recording, AI models)
+**Then** each item MUST display a completion indicator. Microphone and accessibility MUST show grant/settings buttons. Screen recording MUST be labeled "(optional)" with "You can enable this later in Settings." text. The "Continue" button MUST only appear when microphone is granted, accessibility is granted, AND the model manager is ready. Screen recording is NOT required to proceed.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-008: Permission polling at 2-second intervals
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** onboarding setup step or settings window is displayed with missing permissions
+**When** the polling timer runs
+**Then** a 2-second repeating timer MUST poll accessibility and screen recording status. When all checked permissions are granted, the timer MUST self-invalidate. The timer MUST be invalidated on view disappearance.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-009: Permission checker uses test client in test environment
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** the application is running in a test environment (detected by test configuration file path environment variable)
+**When** permission operations are invoked
+**Then** the system MUST automatically select the test client (accessibility returns false, microphone returns denied, void operations are no-ops). The current client MUST be a mutable static property allowing test replacement.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PERM-010: Screen recording relaunch workaround during onboarding
+**Priority:** P2
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user taps the "Enable" button for screen recording during onboarding
+**When** the enable action is triggered
+**Then** the system MUST schedule a deferred relaunch (shell process sleeping 3 seconds then opening the app), call the permission request function, and open the Screen Recording settings pane.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Recording Pipeline
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-001: Happy path pipeline completes all stages in sequence
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application is in the "ready" state with a speech model loaded and hotkey monitor active
+**When** the user presses and holds the push-to-talk chord, speaks, and releases the chord
+**Then** the pipeline MUST proceed through stages in order: hotkey detected, trace created, pipeline acquired, recording started, start sound played, overlay shown ("Recording..."), state set to "recording", hotkey released, recording stopped (after 200ms flush), stop sound played, state set to "transcribing", audio transcribed, text cleaned (if enabled), text pasted, trace completed, overlay dismissed, state returned to "ready", pipeline released.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-002: Pipeline exclusivity prevents concurrent sessions
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the pipeline is currently owned by a live recording session
+**When** a second recording start is attempted (either from hotkey or transcription lab)
+**Then** the live recording attempt MUST be silently skipped with a debug log entry, the performance trace MUST be abandoned, and the transcription lab attempt MUST receive a "pipeline busy" error.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-003: Pipeline ownership released after every run
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a pipeline run has completed (whether successful, empty transcription, or error)
+**When** the pipeline finishes all processing
+**Then** pipeline ownership MUST be released, regardless of outcome, allowing new recording sessions.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-004: State machine transitions follow defined sequence
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application is in any valid state
+**When** pipeline events occur
+**Then** the status MUST transition only through the defined paths: loading -> ready (on model load), ready -> recording (on hotkey press), recording -> transcribing (on hotkey release), transcribing -> cleaning up (if cleanup enabled and available), cleaning up -> ready (on completion), transcribing -> ready (if no cleanup). Status MUST transition to "error" on recording start failure or permission denial.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-005: Empty transcription with short recording silently cancels
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session has completed
+**When** transcription produces no text and the audio buffer contains fewer than 80,000 samples (approximately 5 seconds at 16 kHz)
+**Then** the recording MUST be silently dismissed (overlay dismissed, no "no sound detected" message), the recording MUST still be archived to the transcription lab with nil transcription values, and the performance trace MUST be completed.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-006: Empty transcription with long recording shows no-sound-detected
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session has completed
+**When** transcription produces no text and the audio buffer contains 80,000 or more samples
+**Then** the overlay MUST show "No sound detected" with an orange indicator, the message MUST auto-dismiss after 3 seconds, and no secondary text MUST be present.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-007: Re-entrancy guard prevents double transcription
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a transcription is already in progress
+**When** a second stop-and-transcribe request arrives
+**Then** the second request MUST be silently ignored. The transcription-in-progress flag MUST be cleared via deferred cleanup even if the transcription path throws or returns early.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-008: Recording blocked unless status is ready
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application status is anything other than "ready"
+**When** a recording start is attempted
+**Then** the attempt MUST be silently ignored. If the status is "loading", the overlay MUST show a "model loading" message for 1.5 seconds.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-009: Performance trace lifecycle completes with all milestones
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a push-to-talk or toggle-to-talk recording session is initiated
+**When** the session completes through the full pipeline (recording, transcription, optional cleanup, paste)
+**Then** the performance trace MUST contain timestamps for: hotkey detected, microphone live, hotkey lifted, microphone cold, transcription start, transcription end, cleanup start/end (if applicable), paste start, paste end. The trace summary MUST be written to the debug log as a performance entry. The trace MUST be cleared after completion.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-010: Trace abandoned on pipeline acquisition failure
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a performance trace has been created for a recording session
+**When** pipeline acquisition fails (pipeline busy)
+**Then** the trace MUST be silently abandoned (set to nil) without writing a summary to the debug log.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-011: 200ms flush delay preserves final audio
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session is in progress and audio is being captured
+**When** recording stops
+**Then** the system MUST wait 200 milliseconds before removing the audio tap and stopping the engine. The stop sequence MUST proceed: remove tap, stop engine, fire recording-stopped callback, copy and return buffer.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-012: Recording start failure triggers cleanup and error state
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the application is in the "ready" state and a recording is initiated
+**When** the audio engine fails to start (no input device, converter failure, or engine error)
+**Then** the system MUST cancel the OCR prefetch, release pipeline ownership, abandon the performance trace, set an error message, and transition status to "error".
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-013: Push-to-talk upgrade to toggle-to-talk
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** push-to-talk is the active recording action
+**When** the user presses additional keys to complete the toggle-to-talk chord
+**Then** the chord engine MUST fire a "restart recording" action. If fewer than 16,000 samples have been accumulated, the audio buffer MUST be cleared. If 16,000 or more samples have accumulated, the buffer MUST be preserved.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-014: Overlay message replacement and conditional dismissal
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the recording overlay is displaying a message
+**When** a new message is shown or a conditional dismiss is requested
+**Then** showing a new message MUST replace the current message (cancelling any pending auto-dismiss timer). The conditional dismiss method MUST only dismiss if the currently displayed message matches the specified message.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-015: Cleanup failure does not prevent paste delivery
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup model is unavailable or produces unusable output
+**When** the pipeline reaches the cleanup stage
+**Then** the pipeline MUST fall back to deterministic corrections only, MUST flag the result as fallback, and MUST proceed to paste delivery with whatever text is available.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-016: Lab archive failure does not interrupt pipeline
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session completes with valid transcription
+**When** archiving the recording to the transcription lab fails
+**Then** the error MUST be logged but MUST NOT interrupt the pipeline or affect paste delivery.
+
+---
+
+## AC-PIPE-017: Application initialization sequence
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the application launches for the first time after onboarding is complete
+**When** the main view appears
+**Then** the system MUST check microphone permission, check accessibility/input monitoring permissions, pre-warm the audio engine, start paste target tracking, load the speech model, start the hotkey monitor, and refresh the cleanup model state.

@@ -0,0 +1,101 @@
+# Acceptance Criteria: Speech Transcription
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-001: Model catalog offers exactly four models with correct properties
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the speech model catalog is initialized
+**When** the available models are queried
+**Then** exactly four models MUST be present: "Speed" (openai_whisper-tiny.en, ~75 MB, Whisper), "Accuracy" (openai_whisper-small.en, ~466 MB, Whisper), "Multilingual" (openai_whisper-small, ~466 MB, Whisper), "Parakeet v3" (fluid_parakeet-v3, ~1.4 GB, FluidAudio). The default model MUST be "Accuracy" (openai_whisper-small.en).
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-002: Model manager state machine transitions correctly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the model manager is in any state (idle, loading, ready, error)
+**When** a model load is requested
+**Then** the state machine MUST follow defined transitions: idle -> loading on request, loading -> ready on success, loading -> loading (retry) on retryable timeout, loading -> error on non-retryable failure or retry failure, ready -> ready (no-op) on same-model request, ready -> idle -> loading on different-model request. Only one retry attempt is allowed after a timeout.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-003: Transcription dispatches to correct backend
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a speech model is loaded and ready
+**When** transcription is requested with a non-empty audio buffer
+**Then** the system MUST dispatch to the Whisper backend for Whisper models or the FluidAudio backend for FluidAudio models. Empty audio buffers MUST return no result immediately without invoking any backend.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-004: Whisper artifact removal strips hallucination markers
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a Whisper backend transcription produces text containing hallucination artifacts
+**When** the transcription result is processed
+**Then** the system MUST remove exactly these seven artifact strings: [BLANK_AUDIO], [NO_SPEECH], (blank audio), (no speech), [MUSIC], [APPLAUSE], [LAUGHTER]. The result MUST be trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. If the cleaned result is empty, the system MUST return no result. Artifact removal MUST NOT be applied to FluidAudio results.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-005: Speaker diarization coordinator created only when conditions are met
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a request to create a recording session coordinator
+**When** the model is checked for speaker filtering capability
+**Then** a coordinator MUST be created only when ALL three conditions are met: the model exists in the catalog, the model supports speaker filtering (FluidAudio backend only), and the FluidAudio engine is loaded. If any condition fails, no coordinator MUST be produced.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-006: Target speaker selection uses chronological first-appearance with duration threshold
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** diarization has produced speaker timeline segments
+**When** the target speaker is selected
+**Then** the system MUST select the chronologically first-appearing speaker whose total accumulated speaking duration meets or exceeds 0.5 seconds. This is NOT simply the speaker with the most total time; first appearance order is the tiebreaker. If no speaker qualifies, no target speaker is returned.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-007: Speaker filtering falls back to full-buffer transcription on failure
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** speaker filtering is enabled and active during a recording
+**When** any stage of the finalization pipeline fails (no spans, no qualifying speaker, insufficient audio, extraction failure, empty transcription)
+**Then** the system MUST fall back to full-buffer transcription via the serialized transcriber. The diarization summary MUST be preserved for archival regardless of whether filtering produced a usable result.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-008: Transcription access is serialized
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** multiple transcription requests arrive concurrently
+**When** the transcriber processes them
+**Then** only one transcription MUST execute at a time, enforced by a serialization mechanism. Empty buffers MUST be rejected before entering the serialization gate.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-009: Retry on timeout with 500ms delay and single attempt
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a model load fails with a timeout error (URL subsystem "timed out" code or description containing "timed out")
+**When** the system evaluates retry eligibility
+**Then** the system MUST clear loaded model instances, wait 500ms, and retry exactly once. If the retry also fails, the system MUST transition to the error state.
+
+---
+
+## AC-STR-010: Per-chunk diarization errors do not abort overall diarization
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the diarizer is processing real-time audio chunks during a recording
+**When** the diarizer throws an error on an individual chunk
+**Then** the error MUST be caught and logged, and processing MUST continue with subsequent chunks. Individual chunk failures MUST NOT abort the overall diarization.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Text Cleanup
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-001: Full cleanup pipeline produces cleaned text through all six stages
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** raw transcription text is available and cleanup is enabled
+**When** the cleanup pipeline is invoked
+**Then** the text MUST proceed through six stages in order: (1) prompt resolution, (2) pre-cleanup deterministic corrections, (3) XML input formatting with USER-INPUT tags, (4) LLM inference with the resolved prompt and temperature 0.1, (5) output sanitization (reasoning block stripping and whitespace trimming), (6) post-cleanup deterministic corrections. The result MUST contain final cleaned text, performance metrics, optional diagnostic transcript, and a fallback flag.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-002: Fallback to deterministic corrections on any LLM failure
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup pipeline is processing transcription text
+**When** any error occurs during LLM inference (backend unavailable, unusable output, any other error)
+**Then** the system MUST return the pre-cleanup corrected text with post-cleanup corrections applied, set the fallback flag to true, and include the diagnostic transcript (if available). The pipeline MUST never fail to produce output.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-003: Prompt assembly includes correction hints and OCR context when available
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup pipeline is assembling the system prompt
+**When** preferred transcriptions, misheard replacements, and/or OCR window context are available
+**Then** correction hints MUST be formatted in a CORRECTION-HINTS section (preferred transcriptions as bullet points, misheard replacements as arrow-separated pairs). If window context is enabled and available, OCR text MUST be truncated to 4000 characters and included in OCR-RULES and WINDOW-OCR-CONTENT sections. If context is disabled or unavailable, no OCR sections MUST be present.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-004: LLM output sanitization strips reasoning blocks
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the LLM produces raw output that may contain reasoning blocks
+**When** the output is sanitized
+**Then** the system MUST remove all matched pairs of opening/closing reasoning tags (case-insensitive), strip orphan leading reasoning tags with no closing tag, and trim leading/trailing whitespace.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-005: Cleanup model lifecycle state machine transitions correctly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup model manager is in any state (idle, downloading, loading, ready, error)
+**When** model lifecycle events occur (load requested, download completes, initialization succeeds/fails, unload requested)
+**Then** the state MUST transition through defined paths: idle -> downloading/loading on load request, downloading -> loading on download complete, loading -> ready on success, loading -> error on failure, ready -> idle on unload, ready -> downloading/loading on different model request. When a load is requested while another is in progress, the requester MUST wait by polling at 10ms intervals.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-006: Cleanup disabled returns raw text unchanged
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup feature is disabled in settings
+**When** the pipeline reaches the cleanup decision point
+**Then** the raw transcription text MUST be returned unchanged with no cleanup attempted. The cleanup model SHOULD be unloaded from memory.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-007: Post-paste learning infers narrow high-confidence corrections
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** text has been pasted into a target application and learning is enabled
+**When** the user makes corrections to the pasted text
+**Then** the learning coordinator MUST observe the focused element text via the accessibility API, infer replacement corrections only when they are narrow and high-confidence, and store them as new misheard replacement rules. If the polling window expires without a stable correction, learning MUST gracefully abort.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-008: Deterministic correction engine uses protect-replace-restore algorithm
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the correction store contains preferred transcriptions and misheard replacements
+**When** deterministic corrections are applied to transcription text
+**Then** all matching misheard words MUST be replaced and all preferred transcriptions MUST be preserved exactly. Corrections MUST succeed deterministically without any external dependency.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-009: Default cleanup prompt contains required rules
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** no custom prompt has been configured by the user
+**When** the built-in default prompt is used
+**Then** the prompt MUST instruct the LLM to: delete specified filler words, handle self-correction phrases ("scratch that", "never mind", "no let me start over"), fix obvious errors, preserve deliberate phrasing, properly punctuate, reproduce the entire transcript, and MUST NOT delete sentences, remove context, or summarize.
+
+---
+
+## AC-CLN-010: Unusable model output detected and handled
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the cleanup model returns output
+**When** the output after trimming is either empty or consists solely of "..."
+**Then** the system MUST treat this as unusable output, return the pre-cleanup corrected text with fallback flag set to true, and include the diagnostic transcript containing the raw unusable output.

+# Acceptance Criteria: Transcription Lab
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-001: Lab entry captures complete recording session snapshot
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session completes
+**When** the session is archived to the transcription lab
+**Then** the lab entry MUST contain all fourteen fields: unique identifier, creation timestamp, audio file name, audio duration, optional screen context, optional original transcription, optional corrected transcription, speech model identifier, cleanup model display name, cleanup fallback flag, and three speaker filtering flags (enabled, executed, used fallback) plus optional diarization summary.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-002: Lab store enforces 50-entry capacity with oldest-first pruning
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the transcription lab store has reached its maximum capacity
+**When** a new entry is inserted
+**Then** entries beyond the 50-entry limit MUST be pruned oldest-first. For each pruned entry, the associated audio file MUST be deleted (ignoring deletion failures) and the timing record MUST be removed.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-003: Lab store entries sorted newest-first
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** multiple recording sessions have been archived
+**When** entries are loaded from the store
+**Then** the result MUST be sorted in reverse chronological order (newest first).
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-004: Transcription re-run acquires pipeline lock and loads correct model
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the user initiates a transcription re-run in the lab
+**When** the re-run executes
+**Then** it MUST acquire the pipeline lock (throwing "pipeline busy" if unavailable), load the archived audio buffer (throwing "missing audio" if empty), load the requested speech model, transcribe the audio (throwing "transcription failed" if nil), and return the raw transcription. The pipeline lock MUST be released in a deferred block regardless of outcome.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-005: Cleanup re-run assembles full prompt with archived context
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the user initiates a cleanup re-run in the lab
+**When** the re-run executes
+**Then** it MUST acquire the pipeline lock, build the full cleanup prompt from: the base prompt, the entry's archived screen context, the correction store's preferred transcriptions and misheard words, and the screen context inclusion toggle. It MUST execute cleanup with the assembled prompt and return corrected text, fallback indicator, and diagnostic transcript.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-006: Destructive error recovery on corrupt lab data
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the lab store's index or timings JSON file is corrupted
+**When** the system attempts to load entries or timings
+**Then** the system MUST delete ALL three storage artifacts (index file, timings file, and entire audio directory) and return empty results. There is no partial recovery -- corruption in one file destroys all lab data.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-007: Lab entry serialization handles optional fields correctly
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a lab entry with various optional fields set or nil
+**When** the entry is serialized and deserialized
+**Then** the screen context MUST be serialized as a plain "windowContents" string (not the full structured type). Optional text fields MUST use conditional encoding (omitted when nil). The diarization summary MUST round-trip through its own structured encoding. The three boolean speaker filtering fields MUST always be serialized.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-008: Lab controller publishes experiment state for UI binding
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** the lab controller is managing experiment execution
+**When** an experiment is running or complete
+**Then** the controller MUST publish: running stage (nil when idle, transcription, or cleanup), experiment raw transcription, experiment corrected transcription, experiment durations, latest cleanup transcript, error message. Model selection changes MUST fire synchronizer callbacks back to the application.
+
+---
+
+## AC-LAB-009: Insert operation writes audio file atomically
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Code inspection
+
+**Given** a new recording is being archived to the lab
+**When** the insert operation executes
+**Then** it MUST create the audio subdirectory if needed, write the audio data file atomically, load existing entries and remove duplicates, append the new entry, enforce capacity, and write the updated index and timings as JSON atomically.

+# Acceptance Criteria: UI Chrome
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-001: Menu bar icon reflects application status correctly
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the application is running as a menu bar item
+**When** the application status changes
+**Then** the menu bar icon MUST display: custom red-tinted image (original rendering) when recording, ellipsis-in-circle system symbol in orange when loading, warning triangle system symbol in yellow when error, custom image with template rendering (adapts to light/dark) for ready/transcribing/cleaning up.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-002: Menu bar dropdown shows correct items after onboarding
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** onboarding has been completed
+**When** the user opens the menu bar dropdown
+**Then** the dropdown MUST include: "Settings..." button, "Debug Log..." button, version label "Ghost Pepper v{version}", status line reflecting current state (loading/recording/transcribing/cleaning up; hidden when ready or error), download progress bar when applicable, error message in red when present, context-sensitive recovery buttons for error messages mentioning "Input Monitoring" / "Accessibility" / "Microphone", "Check for Updates" button, divider, and "Quit" button with Cmd+Q.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-003: Settings window has correct structure and six sidebar sections
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user opens the settings window
+**When** the window appears
+**Then** it MUST have initial size 960x720, minimum size 900x680, title "Ghost Pepper Settings", horizontal split with sidebar (250pt min, 270pt ideal/max) and scrollable detail area. Sidebar MUST list six sections in order: Recording, Cleanup, Corrections, Models, History, General. Default selection MUST be Recording.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-004: Recording overlay displays correct messages during pipeline stages
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Automated test
+
+**Given** a recording session is in progress
+**When** the pipeline transitions through stages
+**Then** the overlay MUST show: "Recording..." with pulsing red dot during recording, "Transcribing..." with pulsing blue dot during transcription, "Cleaning up..." with pulsing blue dot during cleanup, "No sound detected" with pulsing orange dot and 3-second auto-dismiss when no speech detected in long recording, "Copied to clipboard" with "Cmd+V to paste" secondary text and 3-second auto-dismiss on clipboard fallback, "Learned correction" with "{wrong} -> {right}" secondary text and green checkmark on learned correction.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-005: Overlay panel is non-interactive and appears on all desktops
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the recording overlay is shown
+**When** the overlay panel is created
+**Then** it MUST use non-activating and borderless style, transparent background, floating window level, shadow enabled, MUST ignore all mouse events (non-interactive), MUST join all spaces and be classified as full-screen auxiliary.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-006: Onboarding wizard four-step flow
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the application launches for the first time (onboarding not completed)
+**When** the onboarding wizard is shown
+**Then** it MUST display four steps in sequence: (1) Welcome with app icon, title, tagline, privacy badge, and "Get Started" button; (2) Setup with microphone, accessibility, screen recording (optional), and AI models rows with grant/status indicators and "Continue" gated on mic + accessibility + model ready; (3) Try It with hotkey test recording instruction and key caps; (4) Done with checkmark, "You're All Set!", menu bar mockup, capability list, and "Start Using Ghost Pepper" button. The app MUST activate as regular (visible in Dock) during onboarding.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-007: Pre-onboarding menu bar shows only setup and quit
+**Priority:** P0
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** onboarding has not been completed
+**When** the user opens the menu bar dropdown
+**Then** the dropdown MUST show only a "Show Setup Window" button and a "Quit" button.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-008: Settings recording section has permissions, shortcuts, input, and test dictation
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user navigates to the Recording section in settings
+**When** the section content is displayed
+**Then** it MUST include: a Permissions card (only when accessibility or input monitoring is missing) with grant buttons and status indicators; a Shortcuts card with push-to-talk and toggle-to-talk recorder widgets; an Input card with microphone picker, "Play sounds" toggle, and "Ignore other speakers" toggle (disabled when model lacks speaker filtering); a Test dictation card with start/stop button, recording indicator, transcription progress, and result display.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-009: Settings cleanup section includes prompt editor and context toggles
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user navigates to the Cleanup section in settings
+**When** the section content is displayed
+**Then** it MUST include: an "Enable cleanup" toggle with error display when in error state; a "Cleanup prompt" card with editable text area and "Reset to Default" button; a Context card with "Use frontmost window OCR context" toggle and "Learn from manual corrections after paste" toggle, each showing screen recording recovery view when permission is missing.
+
+---
+
+## AC-UI-010: Transcription lab detail view supports experiment re-runs
+**Priority:** P1
+**Verification:** Manual verification
+
+**Given** the user selects a recording entry in the History section
+**When** the detail view is displayed
+**Then** it MUST show: a "Back to recordings" button, audio metadata with play button (disabled for non-WAV), diarization visualization if available, original transcription, rerun transcription with model picker and run button, cleanup section with inline prompt editor and model picker, and diff views. Run buttons MUST show progress spinners while running and be disabled during operations. Errors MUST display in red.
